# BYTES and MachinePlan Progress Ledger

This ledger tracks the active `/goal` implementation of BYTES plus the typed
MachinePlan migration.

## Contract

- Objective file: `/home/martinkavik/.codex/attachments/2c841483-c47e-4594-8047-2c564dc04182/pasted-text-1.txt`
- Current source plan: `/home/martinkavik/Downloads/BOON_BYTES_AND_MACHINEPLAN_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
- Current Downloads file SHA-256: `353f0fd0731754a2ab8c3844c922177439035da774c033f479f0368969a3b335`
- Earlier expected BYTES source plan SHA-256: `c1d46df77f32ff2c280c9b8508d6c9479b043060c3c51176fd6dc16ba09f3b7c`
- Adaptation note: the objective names `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_IMPLEMENTATION.md`, but the user explicitly moved the plan back to Downloads. On `2026-06-19`, the file currently at the Downloads path had the older generic `# Implementation Plan` content and hash `353f0fd...`, not the earlier BYTES/MachinePlan plan hash. The active `/goal` objective text above remains the authoritative BYTES/MachinePlan contract until the moved plan file is restored or replaced.

## Phase Status

| Phase | Status | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Phase 0 - Baseline | Complete | Baseline and benchmark evidence are superseded by the current required BYTES/MachinePlan aggregate, Cells release benchmark, runtime hardening/finality, machine-readiness, schema, and default-engine readiness gates. |
| Phase 1 - Plan Boundary | Complete | `crates/boon_plan`, typed IDs, constants, source routes, storage layout, operation regions, dirty/commit/delta plans, capability summaries, `DebugMap`, deterministic plan hashing, `verify_plan`, `compile_typed_program`, and `boon_cli dump-plan` exist. `boon_cli run` now defaults to PlanExecutor, with explicit legacy and compare modes retained. |
| Phase 2 - BYTES Parser | Complete | Parser BYTES/Byte syntax, spans, validation, multiline constructors, comments, focused parser tests, and negative cases are covered by current tests and required gates. |
| Phase 3 - BYTES Type System | Complete | BYTES/Byte types, fixed/dynamic refinements, builtin validation, TEXT/BYTES encodings, resolved constants, numeric range checks, File/read_text and File/read_bytes boundaries, docs, and schema-required negative coverage are covered by the current type-system gate. |
| Phase 4 - Semantic IR | Complete | BYTES/byte AST forms, typed initial values, typed update expressions, resolved constants, fixed/dynamic BYTES metadata, and constant-pool deduplication are covered by the required type-system, negative, dump-plan, and aggregate schema gates. |
| Phase 5 - Real MachinePlan Lowering | Complete | BYTES operations, source payloads, const branches, row aliases, TodoMVC list storage/append metadata, Cells row expressions, and source-derived fields lower to typed MachinePlan operations with `cpu_plan_executor_complete=true` on the required aggregate examples. |
| Phase 6 - CPU PlanExecutor and Parity | Complete | The required BYTES, TodoMVC, and Cells PlanExecutor reports pass with zero runtime AST fallback, zero executable string-path fallback, zero unknown plan ops, current Cells demand-current coalescing, and explicit compare/default-engine readiness coverage. |
| Phase 7 - BYTES Runtime/Storage | Complete | Inline/private BYTES state, fixed byte banks, borrowed storage-profile proofs, root and indexed file read/write boundaries, dynamic path rejection, source-payload negatives, and fixed warm-tick evidence are covered by current aggregate-required reports. |
| Phase 8 - Examples | Complete | Focused BYTES fixtures, TodoMVC asset boundary examples, NovyWave asset boundary examples, and Cells ASCII formula scanning are covered by current aggregate-required example reports and schema validation. |
| Phase 9 - Verification and Performance | Complete | The current gate set requires `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing`, default-engine readiness, Cells full compare, Cells release benchmark, TodoMVC release benchmark reproduction, adversarial tamper checks, runtime hardening/finality, machine-readiness, and recursive schema evidence. |
| Phase 10 - Default Switch | Complete | `boon_cli run` now defaults to PlanExecutor/default-engine mode. Explicit `--engine legacy` still runs the semantic runtime, and `--engine compare` still runs PlanExecutor with the legacy comparison oracle. |

## 2026-07-04 PlanExecutor Currentness And Harness Taxonomy Slice

Status: implemented and focused-verified; broad aggregates still require child
report refresh.

What changed:

- PlanExecutor runtime state now commits root list-derived currentness after list
  mutations. TodoMVC clear-completed no longer leaves stale root aggregate fields
  in PlanExecutor state.
- Legacy comparison acceptance now handles demand-current/list-derived semantic
  delta differences in both directions: legacy may publish currentness deltas
  that PlanExecutor omits, and PlanExecutor may publish accepted currentness
  deltas that legacy omits.
- Demand-current accepted field classification now distinguishes root derived
  ids from indexed row storage fields. This keeps TodoMVC root aggregates and
  Cells indexed fields (`value`, `error`, `display_text`) generic without
  example-specific names.
- `run-plan-scenario-events` schema validation now accepts extra or missing
  demand-current `FieldSet` deltas only when they match the report's accepted
  field-path set.
- BYTES/MachinePlan and native GPU aggregate reports now include compact failure
  taxonomies so stale/schema/product blockers can be separated without dumping
  huge child reports.
- Aggregate refresh instructions are now structured `argv`, not only shell text.
  BYTES child reports whose original evidence came from `boon_cli run ...` keep
  that CLI shape in the refresh queue instead of being collapsed into an
  unusable `xtask ... --report` replay.
- Added `xtask run-report-refresh-queue`, a bounded refresh-queue runner that
  reads aggregate `refresh_commands[].argv`, normalizes the local `xtask` or
  `boon_cli` executable, rejects recursive aggregate commands, supports
  label/limit filters, truncates child stdout/stderr, and emits a compact
  schema-valid report. Use dry-run first when debugging the control plane.
- Native-preview source replay side reports are no longer hidden dependencies
  of native preview E2E. The BYTES aggregate now owns the TodoMVC, Cells, and
  physical TodoMVC replay reports that native preview evidence consumes, and
  refreshes them with canonical PlanExecutor-only `boon_cli run --engine plan`
  commands. This prevents a stale legacy-compare side report from masquerading
  as a native product/rendering failure.
- The native aggregate now exposes those replay reports as an explicit
  `report_dependency_graph` with `consumes-source-replay-report` edges and adds
  upstream refresh commands for stale dependencies. A native preview E2E report
  can no longer silently depend on an old BYTES replay side file.

Focused evidence:

- `cargo check -p boon_report_schema -p boon_plan_executor -p boon_runtime -p xtask`
  passed.
- `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_list_plan_executor_replays_todomvc_clear_completed -- --nocapture`
  passed.
- `cargo test -p xtask cells_plan_compare_readiness_accepts_exact_or_strict_demand_current_contract -- --nocapture`
  passed.
- `target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-default-engine-readiness --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-default-engine-readiness.json`
  passed with `default_engine=plan`, `default_switch_allowed=true`, and all
  default/compare child cases schema-valid.
- `cargo test -p xtask refresh -- --nocapture` passed.
- `cargo test -p xtask bytes_cli_compare_report_replay -- --nocapture` passed.
- `cargo test -p xtask bytes_native_preview_source_replay -- --nocapture`
  passed.
- `target/debug/xtask run-report-refresh-queue target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json --dry-run --label cells-full-engine-compare --report target/reports/bytes-plan/refresh-queue-dry-run.json`
  passed and selected exactly the Cells compare replay:
  `boon_cli run examples/cells.bn --scenario examples/cells.scn --engine compare --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-plan-compare.json`.
- `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/refresh-queue-dry-run.json`
  passed.
- `target/debug/xtask run-report-refresh-queue target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json --label todomvc-native-preview-source-replay --label cells-native-preview-source-replay --label todo-mvc-physical-native-preview-source-replay --output-byte-limit 4096 --report target/reports/bytes-plan/refresh-queue-native-preview-replays.json`
  passed with all three selected reports freshened and `stdout_truncated=true`
  where the underlying CLI would otherwise print huge JSON.
- `cargo test -p xtask native_preview_e2e_declares_plan_executor_replay_dependency -- --nocapture`
  passed.
- `cargo test -p xtask non_preview_native_reports_have_no_source_replay_dependency -- --nocapture`
  passed.
- `target/debug/xtask verify-native-gpu-all --check-existing --report target/reports/native-gpu-all.json`
  now emits `report_dependency_graph.kind=report-dependency-dag-v1`, three
  native-preview source replay edges, and three upstream refresh commands when
  the replay reports are stale for the current worktree.
- `target/debug/xtask run-report-refresh-queue target/reports/native-gpu-all.json --dry-run --label cells-native-preview-source-replay --report target/reports/native-gpu/refresh-queue-upstream-dry-run.json`
  passed and selected the canonical PlanExecutor replay command:
  `boon_cli run examples/cells.bn --scenario examples/cells.scn --engine plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-scenario-events-full.json`.

Current broad-aggregate state:

- `target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  still fails honestly because required child reports are stale for the current
  commit/worktree/binaries.
- The new BYTES aggregate taxonomy reports `0` structural schema failures, `0`
  status failures, `0` command failures, `0` measurement-mode failures, `0`
  artifact stale failures, and `0` PlanExecutor fallback failures. The blocking
  counts are freshness: stale git/worktree/binary identity on old child reports.
- The latest aggregate has `66` required child reports. The three native-preview
  source replay reports are aggregate-owned, schema-valid, and refresh with
  `--engine plan`. After local code changes they may be stale by worktree
  fingerprint, but both the BYTES aggregate and the native aggregate now expose
  bounded refresh commands for them.
- The latest aggregate refresh queue contains `62` refresh-debt children and
  `0` true-blocker children. Treat this as control-plane debt until targeted
  refreshes prove a fresh product/runtime failure.

Harness/control-plane architecture debt to cut early:

- Every verifier-consumed side report must be owned by exactly one aggregate or
  manifest-like dependency DAG. Hidden side reports are forbidden because they
  make stale evidence look like current product bugs.
- `run-report-refresh-queue` is the canonical way to refresh aggregate children
  during unattended work. Direct `boon_cli run --report ...` currently prints
  the full report to stdout; changing that CLI behavior needs compatibility
  work, so use bounded queue output until an explicit `--print-report` /
  quiet-output contract exists.
- Aggregates should eventually consume compact summary sidecars or indexed
  freshness metadata instead of reparsing large child reports only to classify
  refresh debt. Current `--check-existing` on the broad BYTES aggregate can
  still take minutes when many large reports are present.
- Legacy comparison reports are useful as explicit oracles, but native preview
  product gates should not depend on legacy-compare side reports. Native source
  replay evidence should use PlanExecutor-only reports unless the gate is
  explicitly testing legacy parity.
- Queue reports should remain small and schema-valid, with truncation and
  selected labels recorded. Do not dump megabyte child reports into the agent
  conversation when compact aggregate taxonomy can answer the same question.
- The next harness cut should make `run-report-refresh-queue` closed-loop:
  after refreshing selected children, rerun the owner aggregate and report
  before/after refresh debt plus any selected labels that did not burn down.

Next required BYTES work:

- Use `run-report-refresh-queue` with label/limit filters to refresh required
  child reports with the current `boon_cli` and `xtask` binaries, then rerun
  the BYTES aggregate.
- If any refreshed child fails with a non-freshness taxonomy count, fix that
  child before treating the aggregate as passing.
- Do not re-open Cells runtime micro-optimization from stale BYTES aggregate
  output alone; use focused Cells/runtime reports if a fresh product or semantic
  regression appears.

## Baseline Failure Classification

- `boon_cli`: `default_scenario_uses_manifest_and_parser_not_text_substrings` currently expects default scenario lookup for a copied TodoMVC-looking file path and receives a manifest error. Treat as pre-BYTES baseline failure.
- `boon_ir`: seven current failures around newer value-expression lowering shape, inline empty row/list typing, nested `THEN/WHEN`, NovyWave source wrapping, and TodoMVC source payload expectations. Treat as pre-BYTES baseline failures.
- `boon_native_playground`: multiple Cells/dev-window tests fail before a NovyWave manual workflow stack overflow aborts the target. Treat as pre-BYTES baseline failure and do not claim native GPU readiness from this run.
- `boon_parser`: two list-memory detection tests fail because expected `items` list memories are absent. Treat as pre-BYTES baseline failure.
- `boon_runtime`: Cells scenario, developer summary, and observed Cells source tests fail before a NovyWave bridge cursor stack overflow aborts the target. Treat as pre-BYTES baseline failure.
- `verify-report-schema`: currently fails because an existing headed scenario report is under the report tree but is not registered as an accepted report shape.
- `audit-goal-readiness`: at Phase 0 the objective required this command, but
  then-current repo behavior rejected it as a removed legacy Ply/COSMIC audit.
  This baseline conflict is superseded by the active BYTES/MachinePlan
  `audit-goal-readiness` preflight added later in the Phase 9/10 readiness
  slice.
- Release benchmark baseline:
  - TodoMVC has usable linked speed evidence at `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-bench-speed.json`, but the wrapper benchmark report is schema-invalid in current HEAD.
  - Cells has no usable benchmark report because its scenario fails before measurement completes.
- The raw Phase 0 and subagent JSON artifacts were normalized into diagnostic report envelopes so they no longer become the first `verify-report-schema` failure. The invalid TodoMVC benchmark wrapper was preserved as `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-bench.raw`, with a schema-friendly wrapper at `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-bench-baseline.json`.

## Initial Architecture Findings

- Subagent report: `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/architecture-scout.json`
- Subagent report: `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/bytes-language-scout.json`
- Subagent report: `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase1-integration-scout.json`
- Subagent report: `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase1-report-schema-scout.json`
- The current source-to-runtime path is parser AST to `boon_ir::TypedProgram` to private runtime planning in `boon_runtime`.
- Existing runtime planning already contains a private `CompiledProgram` and runtime-plan reporting surface. Phase 1 should extract or adapt this shape into `crates/boon_plan` rather than inventing a second executable ABI disconnected from current runtime behavior.
- Bridge/runtime byte support already exists in host-facing layers: `boon_bridge::BridgeSchemaShape::Bytes`, `BridgeValue::Bytes`, `boon_runtime::RuntimeBytes`, `RuntimeBytesPayload`, and bytes columns. This is groundwork, not a source-language BYTES implementation.
- The source language/typechecker/IR still lack a real `BYTES` type and byte literal forms.

## Decisions

### ADR-BMP-0001: Contract path adaptation

The primary implementation contract is currently outside the repo at
`/home/martinkavik/Downloads/BOON_BYTES_AND_MACHINEPLAN_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
because the user explicitly moved it there. This ledger records progress
repo-locally without recreating the moved plan file.

### ADR-BMP-0002: Treat existing byte runtime support as groundwork only

Existing bridge/runtime bytes storage does not satisfy the objective's source
language, type-system, IR, MachinePlan, or PlanExecutor requirements. It may be
reused, but completion requires parser/typechecker/IR/plan/runtime integration.

### ADR-BMP-0003: Plan boundary should reuse current compiled-runtime knowledge

The first MachinePlan slice should map current semantic/runtime concepts into a
public, deterministic `boon_plan` crate, using current runtime-plan tables as a
compatibility reference. Avoid a parallel plan model that cannot execute current
TodoMVC/Cells behavior.

### ADR-BMP-0004: Readiness command conflict and replacement

The goal requires `cargo xtask audit-goal-readiness`, but current HEAD rejects
that command as removed. The conflict is recorded as baseline evidence. Later
verification must not fake a pass; it must either implement a current equivalent
or document the active native GPU gates that replace the removed audit.

Update: the Phase 9/10 readiness slice implements the current equivalent as an
active BYTES/MachinePlan blocker-reporting preflight. Native GPU gates remain
separate and must not be used as fake BYTES/MachinePlan completion evidence.

## Open Phase 0 Work

- Decide whether Phase 0 exit can be considered satisfied with classified

  before Phase 1 is allowed to proceed.
- Add `phase0-baseline.json` fields for the current external source-plan path
  and hash in a follow-up report, because the raw baseline was captured while
  the plan file had temporarily been restored in-repo.

## Phase 3 BYTES Builtin Argument Validation

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T12:26:27+02:00`

Status: partial Phase 3 type-system evidence; complete for the focused v1
BYTES builtin argument-validation slice.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed stale `xtask` child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- Added BYTES-scoped compiler validation for v1 builtin arguments. The
  typechecker now rejects missing required BYTES/TEXT inputs, missing required
  operands such as `index`, `byte_count`, `offset`, `value`, `needle`, and
  `prefix`/`suffix`, and unknown named arguments on BYTES builtins.
- `Text/to_bytes` and `Bytes/to_text` now require an explicit
  `encoding: Utf8|Ascii`; invalid encodings such as `Middle` are compiler
  diagnostics.
- Call-form BYTES inputs are type-checked through the existing builtin
  compatibility path, including `Bytes/length(input: TEXT { ... })`.
- Existing valid pipe and call forms remain accepted, including
  `Bytes/concat(input: ..., with: ...)`, `Bytes/equal(left: ..., right: ...)`,
  and dynamic numeric expressions such as Cells `Bytes/slice(... byte_count:
  inner_length)`.
- Extended `verify-bytes-negative` with schema-required compiler cases for
  bad call-form input types, missing BYTES call inputs, missing BYTES operands,
  invalid/missing text-byte encodings, and unknown BYTES builtin arguments.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `negative_case_count=60`
  - New schema-required case IDs:
    - `typecheck-bytes-call-input-type`
    - `typecheck-missing-bytes-call-input`
    - `typecheck-missing-bytes-required-arg`
    - `typecheck-invalid-bytes-to-text-encoding`
    - `typecheck-missing-bytes-to-text-encoding`
    - `typecheck-invalid-text-to-bytes-encoding`
    - `typecheck-missing-text-to-bytes-encoding`
    - `typecheck-unknown-bytes-builtin-argument`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass=true`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`

## Phase 3 BYTES Result-Length Refinement and Ambiguity Rejection

Recorded at: `2026-06-22T04:20:00+02:00`

Status: partial Phase 3 type-system evidence; complete for the focused static
BYTES builtin result-length refinement and ambiguous-argument rejection slice.

Base/current commit: `6494b54e488dc63cfb39d0e1d50830f1283b80dc`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`

What changed:

- Added contextual BYTES result typing in the typechecker. Static
  fixed-length-preserving/derivable builtins no longer collapse to dynamic
  BYTES when the compiler already knows enough:
  - `Bytes/set`, `Bytes/write_unsigned`, and `Bytes/write_signed` preserve the
    input BYTES type.
  - `Bytes/slice`, `Bytes/take`, and `Bytes/zeros` return fixed BYTES when
    `byte_count` / `length` / `count` is a numeric literal.
  - `Bytes/drop` returns fixed BYTES when both the input length and count are
    statically known and the count is in range.
  - `Bytes/concat` returns fixed BYTES when both operands are fixed-length
    BYTES; otherwise it remains dynamic.
- Ambiguous positional arguments for BYTES boundary builtins are now compiler
  errors. BYTES builtins must use named arguments, with pipe input reserved for
  the actual receiver.
- `Bytes/zeros` now rejects both named `input:` and piped input forms because
  it is a constructor, not a BYTES-transforming operation.
- Extended `verify-bytes-negative` and report schema required cases with:
  - `typecheck-bytes-zeros-rejects-pipe`
  - `typecheck-bytes-rejects-positional-args`

Evidence:

- `cargo test -p boon_typecheck bytes -- --nocapture`
  - `7 passed; 0 failed`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `negative_case_count=67`
  - contains required cases
    `typecheck-bytes-zeros-rejects-pipe` and
    `typecheck-bytes-rejects-positional-args`
  - `typecheck-bytes-zeros-rejects-input` and
    `typecheck-bytes-zeros-rejects-pipe` are now generated from independent
    fixtures, not one shared joined diagnostic string.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json` schema validation passed.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass=true`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/goal-readiness.json`
  - `status=fail`
  - remaining blockers:
    - `8 phases are still partial in the progress ledger`
    - `1 phases are still not started in the progress ledger`
    - `Cells release benchmark wrapper report is missing because TASK-0804A remains blocked by speed budgets`
    - `Phase 10 default switch has not happened; boon_cli run still defaults to legacy`

## Phase 3/4 BYTES Literal-Only IR Constant Readers

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T12:33:19+02:00`

Status: partial Phase 3/4 constant-resolution evidence; complete for the
focused root/scalar BYTES compile-time scalar reader hardening slice.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase3-constant-resolution-audit.json`

What changed:

- Removed the hidden `Utf8` fallback from BYTES text-conversion IR lowering.
  `Text/to_bytes` and `Bytes/to_text` now lower only when an explicit
  `encoding` argument exists and was accepted by the typechecker.
- Added literal-only IR readers for BYTES root/scalar compile-time scalar
  arguments:
  - numeric literals for `index`, `offset`, `byte_count`, `count`, `length`,
    and numeric write `value`
  - byte literals for `Bytes/set(value: ...)`
  - enum/tag/identifier symbols for `endian` and `encoding`
- Left state/path operands on the existing path resolver. This intentionally
  does not force `input`, `left`, `right`, `with`, `needle`, `prefix`,
  `suffix`, dynamic file paths, or row-local operands to become constants.
- Added an IR regression test proving explicit `Text/to_bytes` /
  `Bytes/to_text` encodings lower into `UpdateExpression::{TextToBytes,
  BytesToText}`, and missing encoding fails before IR can invent a default.

Evidence:

- Focused IR tests passed:

  - `bytes_encoding_update_expressions_lower_from_pipe_and_call_forms`
  - `bytes_text_conversion_update_expressions_require_explicit_encoding`
  - broad `cargo test -p boon_ir bytes -- --nocapture`
- Focused MachinePlan lowering tests passed for typed ordered operands:
  - `bytes_get_update_lowers_to_typed_executable_plan_op`
  - `bytes_set_update_lowers_to_typed_executable_plan_op`
  - `bytes_slice_take_drop_updates_lower_to_typed_executable_plan_ops`
  - `bytes_numeric_updates_lower_to_ordered_typed_executable_plan_ops`
  - `text_bytes_conversion_updates_lower_to_typed_executable_plan_ops`

## Phase 3/4 Narrow Resolved-Constant Table

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T12:45:00+02:00`

Status: partial Phase 3/4 constant-resolution evidence; complete for the
focused resolved-constant table and root/scalar BYTES scalar-argument lookup
slice.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase3-resolved-constant-table-audit.json`

What changed:

- Added `TypeCheckReport.resolved_constant_table` with serde defaulting for
  older serialized reports.
- The table is intentionally narrow and only records:
  - unsigned integer literals for BYTES indexes, offsets, and counts
  - signed integer literals for numeric write values
  - byte literals for `Bytes/set(value: ...)`
  - validated closed symbols `Little`, `Big`, `Utf8`, and `Ascii`
- `boon_ir` now builds a `ResolvedConstantLookup` from the typecheck report
  before update-branch lowering and passes it through `update_branches`,
  derived dependency branches, and BYTES root/scalar expression lowering.
- Root/scalar BYTES compile-time scalar arguments now read from the
  typechecker-owned table instead of re-parsing literal AST nodes directly.
- Path/state operands remain on the existing path resolver. Text literals,
  booleans, general symbols, BYTES blobs, row expressions, dynamic file paths,
  and non-BYTES constants are deliberately not included in this table.

Evidence:

- Typechecker evidence:
  - `resolved_constant_table_exports_literal_values_for_ir` proves number,
    byte, `Utf8`, and `Big` entries are exported while TEXT literals stay out
    of the narrow table.
- IR evidence:
  - `cargo test -p boon_ir bytes -- --nocapture` passed with the lookup wired
    into BYTES scalar lowering.
- MachinePlan evidence:
  - Numeric BYTES updates and text/BYTES conversion updates still lower to
    ordered typed executable plan operands after the lookup wiring.

## Phase 5 Cells Row-Expression Deepening

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T06:42:40+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- Added typed row `PlanRowExpression` forms for `ListRef`,
  `ListFindValue`, `ListRange`, `ListMap`, `ListMapItem`, `ListSum`, and
  `ObjectField`.
- Lowered row-local `List/find_value`, `List/find` followed by field
  projection, `List/range`, `List/map`, and `List/sum` without executable
  string paths. Raw `field`, `target`, and map binding arguments are now
  treated as symbols, while `value`, `fallback`, and `new` remain ordinary row
  expressions.
- Fixed row function-body lowering for multiline field bodies and delimiter
  pipelines, so statement chains such as
  `List/range(...) |> List/map(...) |> List/sum()` lower as one expression
  instead of only the first child statement.
- Added WHILE binding-pattern lowering for arms such as `number => number` and
  `index => ...`; the binding is represented by substituting the WHILE input
  expression inside the arm. `TEXT { ... }` WHILE patterns are now canonicalized
  to their literal text payload for row-expression replay.
- Added `*` to row numeric infix lowering. Cells formula parsing eagerly lowers
  all WHILE arms, so multiplication must lower even when the currently selected
  formula does not use it.
- Mirrored the new row list/range/map/sum/find/object-field semantics in the
  PlanExecutor row evaluator and the report-schema expected evaluator.

Root cause found:

- `cell.default_formula` was opaque because row lowering had no typed
  `List/find_value` representation and treated `field: address` /
  `target: value` as row-field reads instead of raw field symbols.
- `cell.value` and `cell.error` were opaque because formula helper lowering
  lost multiline pipeline state, did not bind WHILE pattern variables, and did
  not support the eager `*` branch in `binary_value`.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-dump-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `plan_hash=0100461d6b8069abf9dd8995ef8e16041adf1a1dd415bb0fe93462836ef14c3e`
  - `typed_lowering_executable=true`
  - `executable_string_path_count=0`
  - `runtime_ast_dependency_count=0`
  - `unknown_plan_op_count=0`
  - `graph_rebuild_count=0`
  - `graph_clones_per_item=0`
  - Cells target row ops:
    - op `10` / `cell.default_formula`: `has_expression=true`,
      `expression_kind=list_find_value`, `input_count=3`
    - op `11` / `cell.value`: `has_expression=true`,
      `expression_kind=select`, `input_count=87`
    - op `12` / `cell.error`: `has_expression=true`,
      `expression_kind=builtin_call`, `input_count=87`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Focused formatting gate after the row-expression changes. |
| `env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo check -p boon_plan -p boon_runtime -p boon_report_schema --quiet` | Pass | Focused compile for plan/runtime/schema mirrors. |
| `env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_plan cells_range_list_preserves_typed_bounds --quiet` | Pass | Existing Cells row/range plan test still passes. |
| `env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_plan todomvc_row_bool_not_derived_values_lower_to_typed_inputs --quiet` | Pass | Existing TodoMVC row bool-not plan test still passes. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- dump-plan examples/cells.bn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-dump-plan.json` | Pass | Regenerated the Cells MachinePlan report with row expressions for `cell.default_formula`, `cell.value`, and `cell.error`. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-dump-plan.json` | Pass | Report-schema replay accepts the generated Cells plan report. |

Open findings:

- The new row-expression tree is large and currently duplicates constants/list
  inputs inside each eager WHILE branch. This is correct enough for typed
  lowering but still needs CSE/constant interning and expression DAG sharing
  before it should be treated as the final fast representation.
- Whole-plan CPU executor support remains partial:
  `cpu_plan_executor_complete=false` and
  `cpu_plan_executor_unsupported_op_count=23` in the Cells dump-plan report.
- Full Cells scenario parity and performance evidence are still open Phase 6/9
  work.

## Phase 6 Initial Typed List Storage

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T07:02:00+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/list_initial_plan_ops.bn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- The CPU PlanExecutor initial-state path now initializes typed list storage
  through the existing `plan_initial_list_state` route and reports
  `plan_executor.list_summary`.
- Indexed row state-initialization ops are now validated by `run-plan` instead
  of being rejected as unsupported root scalar storage. Root `state_summary`
  remains scalar-only; row fields live in `list_summary`.
- The report-schema verifier recomputes the expected initial list state from
  the source-derived MachinePlan and rejects `run-plan` reports whose
  `plan_executor.list_summary` does not match.
- `cpu_plan_executor_unsupported_op_count` no longer counts every list slot as
  unsupported by definition. Unsupported count is now based on unsupported
  operation semantics, not on the mere existence of typed list storage.
- Added `examples/list_initial_plan_ops.bn`, a focused list-initialization
  fixture with one inert `SOURCE`, one inert root `HOLD`, and one static typed
  list with two rows.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/list-initial-dump-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `list_storage_count=1`
  - `cpu_plan_executor_complete=true`
  - `cpu_plan_executor_unsupported_op_count=0`
  - `executable_string_path_count=0`
  - `runtime_ast_dependency_count=0`
  - `unknown_plan_op_count=0`
  - `graph_rebuild_count=0`
  - `graph_clones_per_item=0`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/list-initial-run-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `plan_executor.executor=cpu-plan-initial-state-v1`
  - `state_summary.store.ready=true`
  - `plan_executor.list_summary.rows.row_count=2`
  - row `1`: `name=alpha`, `completed=false`
  - row `2`: `name=beta`, `completed=true`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-dump-plan.json`
  - Cells still has `cpu_plan_executor_complete=false`, but
    `cpu_plan_executor_unsupported_op_count` dropped from `23` to `20`,
    matching removal of the old blanket `list_storage_count=3` unsupported
    penalty.

Open findings:

- This does not make Cells whole-plan executable. The remaining Cells
  unsupported count is `20`, now representing actual unsupported operation
  classes such as indexed updates, list projections/views, and broader scenario
  execution, not list storage existence.
- Full indexed Cells interaction parity and performance evidence remain Phase
  6/9 work.

## Phase 1 Work Completed So Far

Files changed:

- `Cargo.toml`
- `Cargo.lock`
- `crates/boon_plan/Cargo.toml`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_cli/Cargo.toml`
- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/Cargo.toml`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- New `boon_plan` crate.
- `PlanVersion`, `TargetProfile`, typed plan IDs, `MachinePlan`, typed source routes, constants, storage layout, operation regions/ops, dirty plan, commit plan, delta plan, capability summary, debug map, deterministic SHA-256 plan hash, and structural verifier.
- `boon_cli dump-plan <source> [--target|--profile ...] [--report ...]`, using `boon_runtime::load_and_lower` so manifest-backed multi-file examples keep current loading behavior.
- Generic schema-friendly diagnostic envelope for `dump-plan --report`.
- Multi-file `source_files` in dump-plan reports, aggregate `source_hash` for parsed source sets, and deterministic `program_hash` from path/module/start-line/source bytes.
- `boon_report_schema` now deserializes embedded `machine_plan`, rejects unknown/non-canonical MachinePlan JSON fields, recomputes `boon_plan::plan_sha256`, reruns `verify_plan`, compares verification status/error count, requires pass dump-plan reports to have `exit_status=0`, and rejects forged plan hashes.
- `boon_plan::verify_plan` now recomputes capability summary counts, including unsupported scalar/list initializers, so `unknown_plan_op_count` and `executable` are not trusted report-only fields.
- Current runtime default remains unchanged.

Phase 1 open findings:

- The plan boundary is not executable yet. Current TodoMVC/Cells plans still rely on unresolved executable refs from string/path-shaped IR slots (`title`, `text`, `address`, etc.).
- Real operation lowering, source payload typing, dirty/commit/delta execution semantics, and no-fallback counters remain Phase 5/6 work.
- `dump-plan` reports are diagnostic. They are not accepted PlanExecutor evidence.
- Dump-plan schema/hashing is stronger than the plan lowering itself: it proves current structural plan reports are fresh and non-executable, not that a typed CPU PlanExecutor exists.

## Phase 2/3/4/7 Slice: Source BYTES Through Typecheck And Legacy Runtime Carrier

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T15:17:00+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_parser/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- Parser AST variants for byte literals and BYTES constructors:
  `ByteLiteral`, `BytesLiteral`, and `BytesSizeSyntax`.
- Parsing and validation for:
  - `BYTES {}`
  - `BYTES[__] { ... }`
  - `BYTES[N] { ... }`
  - explicit-base byte literals such as `16uFF`, `10u255`, and `2u10101010`
  - nested BYTES constructor items
  - malformed base/digits, out-of-range byte values, invalid v1 size syntax,
    missing constructor bodies, spaced byte literals such as `16 uFF`, and
    unexpected trailing tokens after BYTES constructor bodies.
- Typechecker support for:
  - `Type::Byte`
  - `Type::Bytes(BytesType::Dynamic)`
  - `Type::Bytes(BytesType::Fixed(N))`
  - exact fixed-size constructor checks
  - fixed length inference for `BYTES[__]`
  - zero-filled empty fixed constructors such as `BYTES[64] {}`
  - rejecting implicit `TEXT` in BYTES constructors with an explicit
    `Text/to_bytes` suggestion
  - v1 builtin return signatures for the required `Bytes/*` and
    `Text/to_bytes` conversion surface
  - static validation that BYTES numeric read/write operations use
    `endian: Little|Big` and literal `byte_count` values in `{1,2,4,8}`.
  - structural widening for `BYTE`, same-size fixed `BYTES`, differing fixed
    `BYTES`, and mixed `BYTE`/`BYTES` call-site inference.
- IR coverage for new AST nodes in expression classification, node kind/labels,
  argument/value helpers, dependency/search walkers, function-call collection,
  and view-binding traversal.
- Legacy runtime coverage for:
  - cached `RuntimeGenericExpr::Bytes`
  - source-language BYTES literal evaluation to inline `RuntimeBytes`
  - deterministic generic expression artifacts for BYTES
  - traversal of BYTES items in argument-access, free-name, binding-reference,
    expression-tree, and called-function walkers.
- Architecture note: `docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md`.

Important limitations:

- The new `ByteLiteral` evaluates as a number-like scalar in the legacy runtime.
  Final typed byte storage and operation execution must be implemented in the
  MachinePlan/PlanExecutor path.
- Legacy BYTES constructor flattening only accepts inline runtime bytes. Blob
  and page-backed bytes inside constructors are rejected until Phase 7 dynamic
  byte storage and operation bodies exist.
- BYTES builtin runtime bodies are not implemented in this slice.
- MachinePlan reports remain structural and non-executable:
  - TodoMVC `executable=false`, `unresolved_executable_ref_count=9`,
    `executable_string_path_count=9`
  - Cells `executable=false`, `unresolved_executable_ref_count=10`,
    `executable_string_path_count=10`
- No example refactors were done in this slice.
- No default execution switch was attempted.
- `dump-plan` report schema now accepts failing diagnostic reports only when
  they contain a failing `machine-plan-executable` per-step check and
  `capability_summary.executable=false`. The recursive report-schema traversal
  counts these as accounted diagnostic failures, not as proof reports. The
  schema also rejects forged plan hashes and unknown raw `machine_plan` fields.
- The raw `phase2-phase3-bytes-language-auditor.json` subagent artifact was
  normalized into the same schema-friendly wrapper used by earlier scout
  reports; its findings remain under `subagent_report`.

  - `status=fail`

  - `plan_hash=cdff5b7ba3ba02886e9efa03a85abbbd4adf0ab7363c2ef51b556d762f4ec109`

## Phase 5/6/8/9 Slice: BYTES[4] MachinePlan To CPU Initial-State Executor

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T16:39:00+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_plan/Cargo.toml`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/Cargo.toml`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/Cargo.toml`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `examples/bytes_initial.bn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- `PlanConstantValue::Bytes` now carries `byte_len`, SHA-256, and an inline
  payload for small constants (`INLINE_BYTE_CONSTANT_LIMIT = 1024`).
- `ScalarStorageSlot` now records a typed `PlanValueType`, including
  `Bytes { fixed_len: Some(N) }` for fixed source literals.
- `StateInitialize` ops now carry the typed `initial_constant_id` they execute.
- `boon_plan::verify_plan` now checks inline byte payloads against their length
  and SHA-256 and treats hash-only byte constants as non-executable until a
  payload resolver exists.
- `boon_runtime::execute_machine_plan_initial_state` and
  `boon_cli run-plan` now provide a deliberately narrow CPU PlanExecutor slice
  for scalar initial state only.
- The executor accepts source-route metadata plus scalar `StateInitialize`
  regions, rejects lists/indexed storage/update/derived/list/dependency ops, and
  returns public BYTES summaries without exposing inline payload bytes.
- The executor now rejects a nominally executable plan if it does not initialize
  every scalar storage slot or account for every source route metadata op.
- Added `examples/bytes_initial.bn` as a stable minimal fixture:
  `BYTES[4] { 16u01, 16u02, 16u03, 16u04 }`.
- `cargo xtask verify-report-schema` now accepts optional report paths for
  targeted validation without changing the existing full-tree aggregate mode.
- `boon_report_schema` now has a `run-plan` branch and recompiles report source
  files plus `target_profile` into a source-derived MachinePlan. It binds
  `dump-plan` and `run-plan` evidence to that source-derived plan, compares full
  `PlanVerification`, rejects cross-source MachinePlan swaps, and checks
  `run-plan` counters/state summary against the source-derived initial state.

| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting check passed. |
| `cargo check -p boon_plan -p boon_runtime -p boon_cli --all-targets` | Pass | Focused compile check passed. |
| `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` | Pass | Existing native GPU/playground dead-code warnings only. |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- run-plan examples/bytes_initial.bn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-initial-run-plan.json` | Pass | Generated the CPU initial-state PlanExecutor proof report. |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- dump-plan examples/bytes_initial.bn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-initial-dump-plan.json` | Pass | Generated executable BYTES[4] MachinePlan diagnostic report. |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- dump-plan examples/todomvc.bn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-dump-plan.json` | Pass | Command exits 0 as diagnostic; report status remains `fail` because the plan is non-executable. |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- dump-plan examples/cells.bn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-dump-plan.json` | Pass | Command exits 0 as diagnostic; report status remains `fail` because the plan is non-executable. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-initial-run-plan.json target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-initial-dump-plan.json target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-dump-plan.json target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-dump-plan.json` | Pass | Targeted schema validation passed for the current BYTES/MachinePlan reports. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase5-phase6-planexecutor-slice-review.json target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase9-adversarial-report-schema-review.json` | Pass | Targeted schema validation passed for the two new subagent review artifacts. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema` | Fail | Full aggregate still fails on pre-existing `target/reports/native-gpu/headed-scenario-counter-basic-headed.json` unrecognized `status=pass` shape. |

Subagent review and follow-up:

- `phase5-phase6-planexecutor-slice-review`: confirmed the minimal BYTES[4]
  executor slice is honest and zero-counter only for that no-list initial-state
  surface. It found no critical/high issues. One medium runtime proof gap was
  fixed by requiring initialized scalar and source-route counts to match the
  plan. The remaining medium findings are documented limitations, not blockers
  for the minimal slice.
- `phase9-adversarial-report-schema-review`: found one critical and two high
  evidence problems. All three were addressed before this ledger update:
  schema now recompiles report source files to bind embedded MachinePlans to
  source, compares the full `PlanVerification`, and validates `run-plan`
  MachinePlan/counter/state evidence instead of letting it pass as a generic
  proof report.

Exit-gate status for this slice:

- Satisfied only for the minimal `examples/bytes_initial.bn` scalar initial
  state surface.
- Not satisfied for TodoMVC or Cells source-route parity, list validity,
  generation/order, semantic deltas, conflicts, errors, runtime tick execution,
  or default PlanExecutor switching.
- No speedup is claimed from this slice. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 5/6/9 Slice: Typed Source Payload Route To TodoMVC Parity

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T17:19:07+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- `PlanOpKind::UpdateBranch` now records an optional typed
  `source_payload_field`.
- `ValueRef` now includes typed `SourcePayload { source_id, field }` operands.
- `boon_plan` resolves `UpdateExpression::SourcePayload { path }` against the
  branch source instead of treating payload fields such as `text` as global
  string paths.
- `boon_plan` resolves indexed row-scope aliases such as `title`, `edit_text`,
  and `completed` through the row scope of the branch target into typed state
  refs.
- Bare payload names are accepted only when the branch source schema actually
  contains the payload field. The resolver order is exact path, row alias, then
  source payload, so row fields are not accidentally hidden as payloads.
- Added a lowering regression test proving the TodoMVC
  `store.sources.new_todo_input.change -> store.new_todo_text` branch has a
  typed `SourcePayload(Text)` input and zero unresolved executable refs.
- Added a lowering regression test proving TodoMVC row aliases lower to a
  structurally executable MachinePlan with no unresolved executable refs.
- Added `boon_cli run-plan-route` and
  `boon_runtime::run_plan_source_route` for a deliberately narrow CPU
  PlanExecutor route slice:
  - unscoped root source route only
  - one non-indexed `SourcePayload` update branch
  - typed payload field must be present in the route schema and op inputs
  - selected route-surface fallback counters must be zero
  - optional legacy comparison uses the existing `LiveRuntime` event API for
    parity evidence
- Added `boon_report_schema` validation for `run-plan-route` reports. The
  schema recompiles report source files into a source-derived MachinePlan,
  checks plan hash/version/capability summary, reselects the typed route op,
  recomputes the expected state value from `source_event`, verifies zero route
  fallback counters, and requires legacy comparison to pass.
- Removed the temporary generated
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-one-route-legacy.json` probe report because
  it referenced an unstable `/tmp` scenario path.

Subagent input used:

- `phase5-phase6-planexecutor-slice-review` and live subagent follow-up both
  identified the TodoMVC root `new_todo_input.change -> store.new_todo_text`
  branch as the smallest honest route parity slice.
- The follow-up noted the old plan compiler treated `SourcePayload { path:
  "text" }` as an unresolved global path. This slice fixes that compiler-plan
  problem directly.
- The follow-up also confirmed the legacy runtime event
  `store.sources.new_todo_input.change` with text `Test todo` produces
  `store.new_todo_text = "Test todo"` and semantic delta
  `FieldSet:store.new_todo_text`.
- A later independent subagent pass classified the remaining eight TodoMVC refs
  as row aliases (`edit_text`, `title`, `completed`) plus one source payload
  text field. It confirmed there was no separate unknown-op or dependency-edge
  problem and that the correct fix belongs in `boon_plan`.

Exit-gate status for this slice:

- Satisfied for the structural TodoMVC MachinePlan lowering surface: the current
  TodoMVC dump-plan has zero unresolved executable refs.
- Satisfied for one unscoped root TodoMVC source-payload update route only.
- Not satisfied for Cells PlanExecutor parity despite structural
  `dump-plan` success.
- Not satisfied for indexed/list source routes, list validity, generation/order,
  conflicts, errors, full semantic delta plans, performance measurements, or
  default PlanExecutor switching.
- No speedup is claimed from this slice. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 5/6/9 Follow-up: Typed Const Route To TodoMVC Parity

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T17:51:02+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- `PlanOpKind::UpdateBranch` const branches now carry typed
  `update_constant_id` refs to `PlanConstant` entries.
- `boon_plan::verify_plan` now checks that:
  - constant IDs are unique and sequential;
  - scalar initial constant refs resolve;
  - const update refs resolve;
  - constant values match target state storage type where the target is a state
    slot;
  - `SourcePayload` update branches do not also carry const refs.
- CPU `run-plan-route` now executes both supported unscoped root update branch
  kinds:
  - `SourcePayload`, proven by
    `store.sources.new_todo_input.change -> store.new_todo_text`;
  - `Const`, proven by
    `store.sources.filter_active.press -> store.selected_filter`.
- Route reports now expose `expression_kind`, `source_payload_field`,
  `update_constant_id`, and `update_constant_value` in both `route_surface` and
  `plan_executor`.
- Source-payload routes report `update_constant_id=null` and
  `update_constant_value=null`; their output value remains in `state_summary`
  and semantic deltas.
- `boon_report_schema` now validates `run-plan-route` reports for both
  source-payload and const branches against a source-derived MachinePlan. The
  const branch expected value is recomputed from the plan constant, not from the
  report claim.
- Added focused unit tests:
  - TodoMVC const update lowering to enum constant `Active`;
  - tampered const update constant ref rejected by `verify_plan`;
  - source-payload and const route reports accepted;
  - forged route payload field and forged const value rejected.

### Addendum: File/write_bytes Live Negative Matrix

Current commit at time of work: `0f891e5` with a dirty worktree from the
ongoing BYTES/MachinePlan migration.

Files changed in this addendum:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
  - added generated live `File/write_bytes` negative fixtures,
  - added `binary_path` to the write report,
  - added `forged-binary-hash` to the write tamper matrix,
  - switched preserved write negative artifacts to targeted mutation checks
    instead of recursive full-schema validation.
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
  - requires and validates `binary_path` when present,
  - validates the write live-negative matrix by exact IDs, source/scenario
    hashes, detail hashes, selected step ids, and target metadata,
  - validates preserved write negative artifacts by exact expected mutation.
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
  - re-exports the targeted write negative artifact verifier.
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
  - added generic builtin call compatibility checks,
  - added a regression test proving `File/write_bytes` requires BYTES input
    and TEXT path.

### Addendum: File/write_bytes Fabricated MachinePlan Negative Matrix

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this addendum:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- `target/generated/bytes-file-write-negative-reports/*.json`

Implementation:

- `verify-bytes-file-write-plan` now fabricates malformed
  `FileWriteBytes` MachinePlans and verifies they fail
  `boon_plan::verify_plan` through the `file-write-bytes-ops-well-formed`
  check.
- The main write report now includes:
  - `fabricated_plan_negative_case_count`,
  - `fabricated_plan_negative_cases`,
  - per-case base and mutated plan hashes,
  - full serialized `boon_plan_verification`,
  - failed-check IDs,
  - explicit `runtime_executed=false`.
- `boon_report_schema` now recomputes each fabricated mutation from the
  source-derived MachinePlan, reruns `verify_plan`, verifies the mutated plan
  hash, compares the full verifier JSON, and rejects runtime-looking claims
  inside fabricated verifier-only evidence.
- The write report tamper matrix now also rejects:
  - missing fabricated-plan negative evidence,
  - forged fabricated mutated-plan hash,
  - forged expected failed-check IDs,
  - fabricated evidence claiming runtime execution,
  - extra runtime report fields inside fabricated evidence,
  - duplicated fabricated cases.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed `Bytes/zeros`, Hex, and Base64 Root Scalar Slice

Base/current commit:

- `0f891e5`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_encoding_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_encoding_plan_ops.scn`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-encoding-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-encoding-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd54-a0fd-7d33-9bc3-c0438ec0b49b-bytes-encoding-implementation-map.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd54-c9e1-7fa2-9895-17e7914e3de9-bytes-encoding-adversarial-audit.json`

What changed:

- Added typed IR update expressions for `Bytes/zeros`, `Bytes/to_hex`,
  `Bytes/from_hex`, `Bytes/to_base64`, and `Bytes/from_base64`.
- Added MachinePlan expression kinds, ordered operands, CPU support checks,
  fixed-length verification for `Bytes/zeros`, and typed lowering coverage for
  the five operations.
- Added CPU PlanExecutor replay for the five operations. Byte-producing
  operations now return private byte state so later `Bytes/length`,
  `Bytes/get`, and `Bytes/to_hex` consumers prove the bytes are really owned by
  the typed executor, not just by public digest summaries.
- Added report-schema replay with independent hex/base64 codecs. Valid reports
  are recomputed from source, private byte state, and constants; focused schema
  tests reject forged expression kinds and forged byte digests.
- Added `examples/bytes_encoding_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_encoding_plan_ops.scn`. The scenario:
  - `build-bytes`: creates `store.joined` through `Bytes/concat`.
  - `encode-bytes`: encodes committed private BYTES to lowercase hex and
    standard padded base64.
  - `decode-text`: runs `Bytes/zeros`, `Bytes/from_hex`, and
    `Bytes/from_base64`.
  - `inspect-decoded`: consumes decoded/zeroed private BYTES through
    `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/get`, and `Bytes/to_hex`.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- `Bytes/zeros(byte_count: 4)` is constant-only in this slice. Dynamic sizes
  remain future work because the current root-scalar typed executor still
  models this operation through a fixed ordered constant operand.
- `Bytes/to_hex` and `Bytes/to_base64` use one ordered BYTES state operand and
  produce TEXT. `Bytes/from_hex` and `Bytes/from_base64` use one ordered TEXT
  state operand and produce BYTES.
- Hex output is lowercase. Hex decode ignores ASCII whitespace and rejects odd
  length or invalid digits. Base64 uses the standard alphabet with `=`
  padding, ignores ASCII whitespace, and rejects invalid padding/characters.
- Public BYTES reports remain digest/length summaries. Inline bytes are not
  exposed in scenario state or semantic deltas.
- The fixture initializes `store.zeros` as `BYTES[4] { FF FF FF FF }` so
  `Bytes/zeros` proves an actual delta. Earlier zero-initialized diagnostic
  fixture work collided with the existing unchanged-delta policy, which is a
  separate engine behavior and not the proof target for this slice.
- This slice does not expand same-source-event dependency scheduling. Encode,
  decode, and inspect happen in separate source ticks.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed `Text/to_bytes` and `Bytes/to_text` Root Scalar Slice

Date: 2026-06-19

Base/current commit:

- Base/current HEAD while implementing this slice: `0f891e5`
- Worktree was already dirty from the active BYTES/MachinePlan goal.

Files changed for this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_parser/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_text_conversion_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_text_conversion_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- Generated reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/` and
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/`.

What changed:

- Added typed IR update expressions:
  - `UpdateExpression::TextToBytes { path, encoding }`
  - `UpdateExpression::BytesToText { path, encoding }`
- Added HOLD-update extraction for both pipe and call forms:
  - `text_payload |> Text/to_bytes(encoding: Utf8)`
  - `Text/to_bytes(input: text_payload, encoding: Utf8)`
  - `bytes_payload |> Bytes/to_text(encoding: Utf8)`
  - `Bytes/to_text(input: bytes_payload, encoding: Utf8)`
- Added parser operator collection entries for current BYTES builtin names and
  `Text/to_bytes`. This is not syntax change; it keeps parser reports/operator
  coverage aligned with existing builtins.
- Added `PlanExpressionKind::TextToBytes` and
  `PlanExpressionKind::BytesToText`.
- Lowered conversion operations to typed ordered operands:
  `[State(input), Constant(encoding)]`.
- MachinePlan verification now requires:
  - `Text/to_bytes`: TEXT input, BYTES output, one source route, no source
    payload input, no unresolved executable refs, and UTF-8 encoding constant.
  - `Bytes/to_text`: BYTES input, TEXT output, one source route, no source
    payload input, no unresolved executable refs, and UTF-8 encoding constant.
- CPU root-scalar PlanExecutor now executes both conversion ops:
  - `Text/to_bytes` reads TEXT, encodes UTF-8, checks fixed BYTES output length,
    emits a public BYTES summary, and stores the private byte payload in the
    root BYTES sidecar.
  - `Bytes/to_text` reads private/constant-backed bytes, decodes UTF-8, emits
    TEXT, and errors deterministically on invalid UTF-8.
- Legacy scalar equation replay now supports both conversion ops so
  `--compare-legacy` can prove parity for the typed PlanExecutor slice.
- Report-schema replay mirrors both conversion ops from the source-derived
  MachinePlan and private byte state. It rejects forged digest, byte_len,
  inline payload injection, decoded text, expression kind, and encoding value.
- Added `examples/bytes_text_conversion_plan_ops.bn/.scn`:
  - Step `encode-text`: writes `store.encoded` from
    `text_payload |> Text/to_bytes(encoding: Utf8)`.
  - Step `decode-bytes`: consumes committed `store.encoded` with
    `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/to_text(encoding: Utf8)`.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- Encoding is represented as a typed plan constant with `PlanConstantValue::Text`
  and is canonicalized/restricted to UTF-8 by the verifier, executor, and schema
  replay. A future dedicated encoding enum/type may be cleaner, but this slice
  keeps compatibility with current Boon syntax and typechecker behavior.
- The fixture uses explicit `encoding: Utf8`. Existing generic builtins still
  default missing encoding to UTF-8; whether omitted encoding should become a
  compiler error remains a broader language-policy follow-up.
- Public reports still expose only BYTES summaries with `storage`, `digest`, and
  `byte_len`; inline byte payloads stay private to runtime/schema replay.
- `Bytes/to_text` over invalid UTF-8 is a deterministic PlanExecutor error, not
  lossy replacement text and not an accepted proof report.
- This is a root-scalar typed PlanExecutor slice. Indexed BYTES-to-TEXT field
  decoding, byte source payloads, File/read_bytes/write_bytes, final measured
  byte banks, full TodoMVC/Cells parity, Phase 8 example refactors, release
  performance, and default PlanExecutor switch remain open.
- No speedup is claimed. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 5/6/9 Follow-up: TodoMVC Submit Root/List PlanExecutor Proof

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T19:24:36+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- Extended `ListStorageSlot.initial_rows` coverage so record-literal rows carry
  typed row-field IDs for both scoped rows and unscoped static data tables.
- Added deterministic synthetic field IDs for unscoped record-literal list
  fields such as Cells `cells_default_values.address`, `.field`, and `.value`.
  These IDs live in the MachinePlan debug field map and avoid leaving
  executable initial rows with `field_id=null`.
- Added typed `PlanDerivedExpression::BoolNot` lowering for indexed row pure
  `Bool/not` fields. TodoMVC row `not_editing` and `not_completed` now lower
  from typed row state inputs.
- Extended the CPU root-scenario PlanExecutor slice from root-scalar-only to a
  narrow root/list executor:
  - evaluates the typed `SourceKeyTextTrimNonEmpty` derived value for
    `store.title_to_add`;
  - materializes a TodoMVC append row from typed append metadata and typed row
    defaults;
  - allocates hidden key/generation below Boon source;
  - emits `ListInsert`;
  - emits row-scoped `SourceBind` deltas for the new row;
  - derives row `Bool/not` fields;
  - gates duplicate reset branches by the submit key and merges same-value
    candidate update branches as `candidate_update_op_ids`.
- Extended report-schema verification for `run-plan-root-scalar-scenario`
  reports so the schema source-derives root/list replay from the MachinePlan
  and scenario instead of trusting report JSON.
- Added submit-specific tamper tests for derived values, list append keys,
  list summaries, source-bind IDs, semantic deltas, selected-step prerequisites,
  and candidate update IDs.
- Added runtime tests for:
  - `add-test-todo-type,add-test-todo-submit`;
  - `reject-empty-todo-type,reject-empty-todo-submit`.

## Phase 6/9 Follow-up: Root Scalar Multi-Event TodoMVC PlanExecutor Proof

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T18:11:49+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- Added explicit CLI/API surface
  `boon_cli run-plan-root-scalar-scenario <source> --scenario <path>
  --steps <ids> [--compare-legacy] [--target <profile>] [--report <path>]`.
- Added a deliberately narrow CPU executor
  `cpu-plan-root-scenario-v1` that replays selected scenario steps through a
  source-derived MachinePlan and persistent root-scalar storage.
- The executor supports only unscoped root scalar update branches where the
  selected update expression is a typed `SourcePayload` or typed `Const`.
- The executor rejects indexed routes, unresolved executable refs, duplicate
  candidate target states, non-root state targets, list/derived work required by
  the selected source route, and selected scenario steps without a source event.
- Added legacy comparison against one persistent `LiveRuntime` over the same
  selected steps. Accepted proof reports require `--compare-legacy` and require
  legacy state and semantic delta signatures to match the plan replay.
- Added report fields for:
  - selected scenario step IDs;
  - per-step source route and target-state update metadata;
  - `source_payload_field`, `update_constant_id`, and
    `update_constant_value`;
  - final root scalar `state_summary`;
  - zero fallback counters.
- Added report-schema validation for `run-plan-root-scalar-scenario` reports.
  The schema verifier recompiles the source-derived MachinePlan, reparses the
  scenario, independently replays the selected root-scalar update branches, and
  rejects forged state, per-step update values, selected-step order, missing
  legacy comparison, forged top-level semantic deltas, forged legacy delta
  lists, and unsupported list/derived steps.
- Tightened the legacy comparison for this proof from subset matching to exact
  per-step semantic delta signature equality. Extra legacy deltas now make the
  comparison fail instead of being silently accepted.
- Added focused runtime and schema tests:
  - `root_scalar_plan_executor_replays_todomvc_multi_event_subset`;
  - `run_plan_root_scalar_scenario_binds_steps_and_rejects_tamper`.

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-root-scalar-scenario-run-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `plan_hash=3fc3a1148b4bccb528f02521f639df73cbdb560fcd6f8962ad95c8307e483411`
  - `executor=cpu-plan-root-scenario-v1`
  - selected steps:
    `add-test-todo-type,filter-active,filter-completed,filter-all`
  - final state summary:
    - `store.new_todo_focused = true`
    - `store.new_todo_text = "Test todo"`
    - `store.selected_filter = "All"`

  - `legacy_comparison.passed=true`
  - `legacy_comparison.state_match=true`
  - `legacy_comparison.semantic_delta_match=true`
  - top-level `semantic_deltas` are schema-bound to source-derived replay, not
    trusted from the report
  - `runtime_ast_eval_count=0`
  - `executable_string_path_count=0`
  - `unknown_plan_op_count=0`
  - `graph_rebuild_count=0`
  - `graph_clones_per_item=0`

Subagent review and follow-up:

- Read-only subagent `019edb71-5201-7e92-a8eb-c7328db5d9d0` independently
  selected the same next slice: a root-scalar multi-event TodoMVC proof limited
  to explicit non-indexed source-payload and const update branches.
- The subagent explicitly advised not to implement `title_to_add`,
  `key_down`, list/indexed rows, or full TodoMVC scenario parity in this slice,
  because the current MachinePlan still lacks enough executable list/derived
  operation semantics for an honest proof.
- The implementation follows that recommendation by rejecting unsupported
  derived/list steps instead of silently falling back to legacy runtime or
  hardcoded TodoMVC behavior.
- Read-only reviewer `019edb81-dff5-7160-85e5-a5fe566d04ff` found two
  evidence holes before this section was finalized:
  - legacy semantic delta comparison was subset-based, so extra legacy deltas
    could pass;
  - top-level `semantic_deltas` were not schema-bound.
- Both high findings were fixed in code and covered by tamper tests/probes:
  the runtime now requires exact per-step delta signature equality for this
  proof, and report-schema verification independently reconstructs and checks
  top-level `semantic_deltas`.
- The same reviewer noted a medium scope caveat: the current derived/list
  rejection checks direct source inputs, not downstream dependency closure
  through changed state. This follow-up remains intentionally scoped to the
  selected root-scalar subset and does not claim absence of downstream
  derived/list work.

Exit-gate status for this follow-up:

- Satisfied for the selected four-step unscoped root-scalar TodoMVC subset.
- Satisfied for source-derived report-schema verification of that subset,
  including tamper rejection for the current report surface.
- Not satisfied for full TodoMVC scenario parity, `title_to_add`,
  `new_todo_input.key_down`, list creation/deletion/toggle behavior, indexed row
  state, conflict/error parity, Cells PlanExecutor parity, release performance
  measurements, broad aggregate gates, or default PlanExecutor switching.
- No speedup is claimed. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 5/9 Follow-up: Typed TodoMVC Append And Derived Submit Planning

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T18:45:43+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/Cargo.toml`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- Added typed `initial_rows` to `ListStorageSlot` so TodoMVC record-literal
  list initializers are visible in the MachinePlan instead of remaining hidden
  in parser/runtime-only state.
- Added typed append metadata to `PlanOpKind::ListOperation`:
  `PlanListAppend { trigger, fields }`.
- TodoMVC `List/append` now lowers as op `49` with:
  - output `List(0)`;
  - trigger `Field(0)`;
  - row field `title = Field(0)`;
  - `unresolved_executable_ref_count=0`.
- Added `DebugMap.derived_values` so derived field IDs are labelled separately
  from semantic field IDs. This avoids falsely reading `ValueRef::Field(0)` as
  a row/semantic field when it is the derived value `store.title_to_add`.
- Added `PlanDerivedExpression::SourceKeyTextTrimNonEmpty` and lowered
  TodoMVC `store.title_to_add` from its AST shape:
  `key_down.key == Enter`, read `store.new_todo_text`, `Text/trim`, skip empty,
  emit the trimmed title.
- The derived op now carries typed inputs:
  - `Source(1)`;
  - `SourcePayload { source_id: 1, field: Key }`;
  - `State(0)`.
- Added verifier checks:
  - `list-append-refs-resolve`;
  - `derived-expression-refs-resolve`.
- Split MachinePlan capability reporting so whole-plan CPU executor readiness is
  not confused with typed lowering:
  - `typed_lowering_executable=true` means no unresolved typed executable refs
    and no unknown/fallback plan ops;
  - `cpu_plan_executor_complete=true` means the entire plan is supported by the
    current whole-plan CPU executor surface;
  - the legacy `executable` field now mirrors
    `cpu_plan_executor_complete` until it can be removed;
  - narrow route/scenario proofs require typed lowering and then validate their
    selected executor surface separately.
- Added focused plan tests:
  - `todomvc_append_lowers_to_typed_trigger_fields_and_initial_rows`;
  - `verifier_rejects_tampered_append_field_ref`;
  - `todomvc_title_to_add_lowers_to_typed_source_key_trim_expression`;
  - `verifier_rejects_tampered_derived_expression_ref`.

Subagent review and follow-up:

- Earlier read-only reviewer `019edb8a-de7a-79b2-aa22-cc6b79cfacbc`
  identified the exact gap this follow-up addresses: append metadata existed,
  but `title_to_add` lacked typed source-key, source-payload, text-trim,
  non-empty, and skip semantics.
- The same reviewer warned that append execution still needs plan-owned row
  materialization and row-source binding before `add-test-todo-submit` can be
  an honest parity proof.
- A fresh read-only reviewer was started for this follow-up after the code was
  present.
- Fresh reviewer `019edb9d-9a55-7063-ac10-01e0fa30faa2` passed the
  plan-lowering evidence and confirmed executor parity remains unproven. It
  reported one high issue: `capability_summary.executable=true` overstated
  TodoMVC whole-plan CPU executor readiness. This was fixed by adding the
  typed-lowering vs CPU-complete capability split and regenerating reports.
- The same reviewer reported two medium follow-ups that remain open:
  append/initial-row destination columns are still field names instead of typed
  row-field IDs, and `SourceKeyTextTrimNonEmpty` is a narrow pattern recognizer
  rather than a general derived-expression lowering framework.

Exit-gate status for this follow-up:

- Satisfied for typed plan-lowering evidence for TodoMVC initial list rows,
  append trigger/row-field refs, and `title_to_add` source-key/text-trim
  semantics.
- Satisfied for focused verifier coverage and targeted schema validation of the
  regenerated reports.
- Satisfied for honest capability reporting: TodoMVC and Cells now report
  typed lowering complete but whole-plan CPU executor incomplete.
- Not satisfied for CPU execution of `add-test-todo-submit`, generic row
  materialization, `ListInsert` semantic delta emission, row `SourceBind`
  emission, append/reset commit ordering, full TodoMVC scenario parity, Cells
  PlanExecutor parity, performance measurements, broad aggregate gates, or the
  default PlanExecutor switch.
- No speedup is claimed. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 6/9 Follow-up: Scoped TodoMVC Row Toggle PlanExecutor Proof

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T19:52:00+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edbcb-92d1-71e1-ac6b-149205dd93f9-scoped-toggle-parity-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edbcb-aff3-75c2-94ff-1b8d46d7a069-scoped-toggle-adversarial-audit.json`

Implemented:

- Added a generic CPU PlanExecutor path for scoped indexed row update branches,
  currently covering typed indexed `Bool/not` branches.
- Removed the runtime blocker that rejected scoped source routes for the
  root/list scenario executor.
- The executor now resolves a scoped row source event against plan-owned list
  storage by:
  - `target_key` + `target_generation` when supplied;
  - route `address_lookup_field` + `address` when supplied;
  - otherwise scenario harness `target_text` + `target_occurrence`.
- The executor validates row source binding epochs when the scenario supplies
  `bind_epoch` or `source_epoch`.
- The scoped TodoMVC checkbox route now executes static MachinePlan source route
  `source_id=14` while reporting the dynamic row source binding
  `source_binding_id=30`/`bind_epoch=30` for row `key=5`,
  `generation=1`.
- The toggle branch executes plan op `45` generically as indexed `bool_not`
  against row field `completed`.
- Row-local derived bool fields are refreshed after the indexed update. The
  focused proof emits both:
  - `FieldSet:completed`;
  - `FieldSet:not_completed`.
- Added runtime test
  `root_list_plan_executor_replays_todomvc_dynamic_checkbox_toggle`.
- Extended report-schema source-derived replay for root/list scenario reports
  to support scoped indexed Bool/not row updates.
- Extended schema source-event reconstruction with `target_text`,
  `target_occurrence`, `target_key`, `target_generation`, `bind_epoch`,
  `source_id`, `source_epoch`, and `list_id`.
- Added report-schema tamper cases for the scoped toggle proof:
  - missing submit prerequisite;
  - forged row key;
  - forged source binding id;
  - forged row-resolution target text;
  - forged indexed update value;
  - forged row-derived `not_completed`;
  - forged semantic delta value;
  - forged `executed_indexed_update_count`;
  - forged final completed count.

## Phase 6/9 Follow-up: Dynamic TodoMVC Row Delete PlanExecutor Proof

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T20:17:54+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/todomvc_plan_slices.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-dynamic-delete-root-list-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edbe1-a925-7861-97e5-b5cddb635b0e-dynamic-delete-parity-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edbe1-c731-7bd0-951d-c7cfea73671f-dynamic-delete-adversarial-audit.json`

Implemented:

- Added typed list-remove metadata to MachinePlan:
  `PlanListRemove` plus `PlanListRemovePredicate`.
- Lowered TodoMVC remove branches into typed remove metadata:
  - scoped row remove from `todo.sources.remove_todo_button.press` becomes
    `AlwaysTrue`;
  - `clear_completed_button.press` becomes `RowFieldBool` over
    `todo.completed`.
- Added verifier check `list-remove-refs-resolve` and a negative test that
  rejects a tampered remove source reference.
- Added generic CPU PlanExecutor support for scoped `List/remove` when the
  selected source route maps to an `AlwaysTrue` row remove predicate.
- The executor resolves the target row through the existing row-resolution
  path, validates the source binding epoch, emits row-scoped `SourceUnbind`
  deltas for all scoped routes, removes the row from plan-owned list storage,
  emits `ListRemove`, and records `list_removes`.
- Added counters:
  - `executed_list_remove_count`;
  - `emitted_source_unbind_count`.
- Added `examples/todomvc_plan_slices.scn` as a focused plan-slice scenario
  file with dynamic append and dynamic delete steps.
- Added runtime test
  `root_list_plan_executor_replays_todomvc_delete_row` for:
  `add-test-todo-type`, `add-test-todo-submit`,
  `delete-dynamic-test-todo`.
- Extended report-schema source-derived replay for dynamic row removal,
  source unbinds, list summaries, counters, and per-step `list_removes`.
- Extended report-schema tamper coverage for dynamic delete:
  - forged row key;
  - forged source binding id;
  - forged row-resolution key;
  - forged unbound source id;
  - forged top-level semantic delta source id;
  - forged list-remove count;
  - forged source-unbind count;
  - forged final row count.
