## 2026-06-19 — Root List-View Typechecker Input/Output Shape Split

Status: resolved for the two root-list-view fixture failures that remained
after the NovyWave stack fix. This is still a focused engine/typechecker fix;
the broader BYTES/MachinePlan Phase 6 through Phase 10 gates remain incomplete.

Independent audit:

- Root list-view typechecker auditor:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee05b-52ec-71f3-9c15-075c4ac0b0e9-root-list-view-typecheck-audit.json`
  - Confirmed both failing Boon sources were valid.
  - Identified the typechecker bug as conflation of `List/map` input row
    bindings (`row` / `signal`) with mapped output item shapes
    (`label`, `variable_only`, `group_only`).
  - Recommended splitting row input type from mapped output list item type
    without weakening structural typing or rewriting fixtures.

Root cause:

- `canonical_row_scope_type` seeded row-scope argument types from constructor
  return/output shape and the row-scope list binding.
- For field-assigned maps such as `projected: rows |> List/map(...)`, the
  parser records `row_scope.list` as the output field (`projected`), not the
  input list (`rows`).
- Early name bindings often only knew the map input as `LIST<[...]>`, so
  helper paths accepted an open placeholder instead of falling back to the
  concrete list literal shape.
- The result was false diagnostics where `variable_row(row: row)` and
  `current_label(signal: signal)` saw `[label: ...]` output rows instead of
  source rows with `name`, `id`, and `current`.

What changed:

- `function_arg_call_site_type` now seeds user-function parameter inference
  from concrete `List/map(..., new: function(param: item))` input item shapes.
- Row-scope display/inference now derives row input type from the map input
  item and keeps mapped output item type separate for output-list bindings.
- Open list item placeholders are treated as weak evidence; the checker falls
  back to concrete list statement item shapes before accepting an open item.
- Added a focused typechecker regression proving mapped output-only fields
  (`label`, `variable_only`, `group_only`) do not replace input row fields
  (`id`, `name`, `current`).

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee05b-52ec-71f3-9c15-075c4ac0b0e9-root-list-view-typecheck-audit.json`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_list_view_branch_selector_dirty_falls_back_before_field_patch -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Initially reproduced `object is missing field name`; passed after the input/output shape split. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_list_view_change_preserves_reusable_user_function_cache_entries -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Initially reproduced `signal.id` / `current` shape diagnostics; passed after the input/output shape split. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck list_map_function_args_keep_input_shape_separate_from_output_shape --lib -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | New regression failed while helper matching and open-list fallback were incomplete; passed after the final helper changes. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck function_argument_call_site_index_merges_named_positional_and_pipe_calls --lib -- --nocapture` | Pass | Existing call-site merge behavior still works. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- dump-ir /tmp/boon-listmap-*.bn` | Pass | Temporary diagnostic source showed `row_scope.list=projected` and open placeholder input list evidence before the concrete-shape fallback fix. Temporary file was removed. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_list_view -- --nocapture` | Pass | 24 root-list-view-filtered runtime tests passed. |
| `cargo check -p boon_typecheck --lib` | Pass | Focused compile check for the typechecker helper changes. |
| `cargo fmt --all` | Pass | Applied rustfmt. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting clean after rustfmt. |
| `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` | Pass | Existing warnings only. |

Open findings after this follow-up:

- Medium: root list-view typecheck/runtime fixtures in the filtered set are
  now clean, but full workspace tests were not rerun to completion in this
  follow-up.
- Medium: the fix improves type precision for field-assigned `List/map` calls,
  but it does not implement broader lazy/field-granular list materialization or
  PlanExecutor list parity.
- Medium: aggregate BYTES/MachinePlan reports and final default-switch gates
  still need a full refresh.

## 2026-06-19 — BYTES Negative Report Numeric Delta Replay and Aggregate Refresh

Status: resolved for the current BYTES/MachinePlan aggregate evidence refresh.
This is a report-schema/verification fix plus evidence regeneration; the
broader BYTES/MachinePlan objective remains partial because the default
PlanExecutor switch, full workspace test pass, release performance evidence,
and final Phase 10 cleanup are still not complete.

Independent audits:

- BYTES negative delta auditor:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee071-1410-7563-8459-6727d551de95-bytes-negative-delta-audit.json`
  - Reproduced the original
    `run-plan-root-scalar-scenario semantic_deltas do not match
    source-derived replay` failure.
  - Confirmed the root cause was serializer drift in report-schema replay,
    not a MachinePlan/runtime execution bug.
  - Confirmed current runtime reports emit numeric JSON delta values for
    `store.patched_byte = 170` and `store.patched_len = 4`.
- BYTES aggregate negative-flow auditor:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee071-1540-72a3-96dd-e3610dd2ec22-bytes-aggregate-negative-flow-audit.json`
  - Confirmed `bytes-negative-base.json` is an internal artifact of
    `verify-bytes-negative`, not a direct aggregate child report.
  - Confirmed the correct direct aggregate child is
    `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`.
  - Recommended regenerating `verify-bytes-negative` rather than promoting
    the internal base report into the aggregate child list.

Root cause:

- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs` had an older
  `expected_root_semantic_delta_value` contract that stringified numeric root
  scalar semantic-delta values.
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs` already emitted typed JSON values for the
  same root PlanExecutor semantic deltas.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative-base.json`, generated by
  `verify-bytes-negative` as a positive baseline artifact, therefore failed
  source-derived replay validation for the current `bytes_set_plan_ops`
  numeric deltas.

What changed:

- `expected_root_semantic_delta_value` now preserves the typed JSON value with
  `value.clone()`, matching the runtime PlanExecutor root-scenario report
  contract.
- The BYTES set report-schema regression now asserts the relevant root
  semantic-delta values remain JSON numbers, not strings.
- Regenerated all direct BYTES/MachinePlan child evidence reports listed in
  the previous aggregate report, skipping only the Phase 0 baseline report.
- Reran the aggregate `--check-existing` gate; it now passes with 36 of 36
  required reports checked and schema-valid.

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- Refreshed child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee071-1410-7563-8459-6727d551de95-bytes-negative-delta-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee071-1540-72a3-96dd-e3610dd2ec22-bytes-aggregate-negative-flow-audit.json`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative-base.json` | Fail, then Pass | Initially reproduced `semantic_deltas do not match source-derived replay`; after the schema replay fix and report regeneration, the report validated. |
| `cargo test -p boon_report_schema run_plan_root_scalar_scenario_accepts_bytes_set_expression_kind -- --nocapture` | Pass | Regression now asserts numeric root semantic-delta values stay numeric. |
| `cargo test -p boon_report_schema run_plan_root_scalar_scenario_replays_private_bytes_from_concat_across_steps -- --nocapture` | Pass | Private BYTES replay across steps remains covered. |

| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Aggregate checked 36 required reports; every child had `status=pass` and `schema_valid=true`. |

Refreshed `verify-bytes-negative` and `verify-bytes-file-write-plan`, then reran aggregate successfully with 36 of 36 reports valid. |

  `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast`, release performance measurements,
  or default PlanExecutor switch readiness.
- Medium: `verify-bytes-negative` validates and hashes nested base artifacts

  pass over 36 required reports.

  suite, release performance proof, default PlanExecutor switch, or legacy
  execution cleanup.

## 2026-06-19 — BYTES Aggregate Binary Path Binding Hardening

Status: resolved for the immediate report-binding weakness found after the
numeric semantic-delta refresh. This is verifier hardening, not Phase 10
completion.

Root cause:

- Several BYTES/MachinePlan reports generated by `xtask` recorded a
  `binary_hash` but either omitted `binary_path` or used `command_argv`
  starting with `cargo`.
- `verify_common_report_shape` can validate a hash through `binary_path` when
  present, or through `command_argv[0]` when that entry is an executable file.
  For `cargo xtask` reports, the fallback binds the `cargo` launcher rather
  than the actual `target/debug/xtask` binary.
- The aggregate report itself was emitted through the generic
  `write_static_gate_report` helper, which did not yet include `binary_path`.

What changed:

- `verify-bytes-negative`, `verify-bytes-file-read-plan`,
  `verify-bytes-file-write-plan`, `verify-build-bytes-boundary`, and
  `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all` now all report
  `binary_path=/home/martinkavik/repos/boon-circuit/target/debug/xtask` with a
  matching current `binary_hash`.
- The generic static gate helper now emits `binary_path`, so the aggregate
  report and other static xtask gates have stronger binary binding.
- The focused BYTES file-read and build-boundary report emitters now include
  `binary_path` directly, matching the existing negative and file-write
  report shape.
- Runtime/CLI report emitters for `dump-plan`, `run-plan`,
  `run-plan-route`, `run-plan-root-scalar-scenario`, and enriched semantic
  reports now emit explicit `binary_path`, so helper reports generated from
  `xtask` or `boon_cli` no longer rely on `command_argv[0]` fallback binding.
- Regenerated the affected xtask-produced child reports and the aggregate
  after every xtask source change to avoid stale hashes.

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee09f-29f0-7620-aef1-d25f5e802ebd-bytes-report-binding-audit.json`

| --- | --- | --- |

| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-file-read-plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json` | Pass | Regenerated the file-read plan report with explicit `binary_path`. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-file-write-plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json` | Pass | Regenerated the file-write plan report under the current `target/debug/xtask` hash. |
| `cargo xtask verify-build-bytes-boundary --report target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json` | Pass | Regenerated the BUILD-byte-boundary report with explicit `binary_path`. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Aggregate checked 36 of 36 required reports; no invalid child reports; aggregate now records `binary_path` and the current xtask hash. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Report schema accepts the hardened aggregate and validates its binary hash through `binary_path`. |

| `cargo check -p boon_runtime -p boon_cli -p xtask` | Pass | Focused compile check after adding runtime/CLI `binary_path` report fields. |

Independent audit:

  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee09f-29f0-7620-aef1-d25f5e802ebd-bytes-report-binding-audit.json`
  - Found a medium gap: helper `run-plan-*` reports generated under `xtask`
    had valid hashes but no explicit `binary_path`.

    adding `binary_path` to runtime/CLI report emitters and regenerating all
    curated child reports.

  completion gate and does not replace `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast`,
  release performance measurements, or the default PlanExecutor switch.
- Medium: `verify-bytes-negative` validates and hashes nested base artifacts
  during generation, and aggregate validates the direct negative report plus
  nested artifact hashes, but aggregate still does not recursively rerun schema
  validation on every nested base artifact.
- Low: report-schema still allows the older `command_argv[0]` binary-hash

  BYTES/MachinePlan direct children and the named nested helper reports no
  longer depend on that fallback because they now emit explicit
  `binary_path`.

## 2026-06-19 — Full Workspace Gate Rechecked After Aggregate Hardening

Status: open. The focused BYTES/MachinePlan aggregate remains useful current
slice evidence, but the full repository verification gate still fails and the
goal is not complete.

Independent audit recorded:

- Completion blocker auditor:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee0b4-aa79-7d42-a20e-655a471d00c3-bytes-completion-blocker-audit.json`
  - High: Phase 10 default PlanExecutor switch has not started.
  - High: Phase 6 parity is still proof-slice based, not full TodoMVC/Cells
    parity.
  - High: release performance evidence is stale or incomplete, with Cells
    release benchmark evidence missing/failing and available latency samples
    over budget.
  - Medium: the current aggregate is fresh but narrow and should remain
    labelled as curated BYTES/MachinePlan slice evidence.
  - Medium: allocation/copy evidence is weak because copy counters remain
    unavailable and zero-allocation fixed-byte tick proof is incomplete.
  - Medium: report-schema still has a historical `command_argv[0]` fallback,
    even though current BYTES/MachinePlan evidence now emits `binary_path`.

Command run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` | Fail | Ran to completion in the active goal session. Failing targets: `boon_cli`, `boon_ir`, `boon_native_playground`, `boon_parser`, `boon_report_schema`, `boon_runtime`, and `boon_typecheck`. |

Failure clusters observed:

- `boon_report_schema`:
  `run_plan_root_scalar_scenario_accepts_bytes_length_expression_kind` still
  rejects the wrong forged legacy-delta case shape after numeric delta replay
  became typed JSON. This is a focused test/fixture drift and should be fixed
  without weakening numeric semantic-delta validation.
- `boon_runtime`:
  - `compiled_artifact_decodes_cells_generic_derived_runtime_plan_without_ast`
    expected 11 entries and observed 12.
  - `live_runtime_applies_numeric_counter_hold_updates_generically` observed
    `Number(1)` where the test expected string `"1"`.
  - `root_derived_cascade_dirties_unqualified_dependents_same_turn` observed
    `Number(42)` where the test expected string `"42"`.
  - Todo row edit/title route tests failed to update the targeted row through
    generic IR-derived branches.
  - Physical TodoMVC row edit routing left the edited title empty instead of
    `Ship physical TodoMVC edited`.
- `boon_typecheck`:
  `todomvc_type_hints_have_complete_store_and_source_shapes` panicked on an
  unexpected missing type-hint entry.
- `boon_cli`, `boon_ir`, `boon_native_playground`, and `boon_parser` still
  have the previously observed broad failures around default-scenario lookup,
  current value-match lowering shape, source-intent/row-binding behavior,
  list-memory detection, and native playground interactions.

Current interpretation:

- The current BYTES root-scalar PlanExecutor surface is healthier than the full
  repository gate: all observed `root_scalar_plan_executor_replays_bytes_*`
  tests passed in this run.
- The broad failures are not acceptable final state for the objective. They
  should be handled as real blockers or explicitly classified with current
  evidence before any final completion claim.
- The next small fix should target a high-confidence test/fixture drift only
  if doing so preserves the architecture contract; row/source routing and type
  propagation failures need root-cause analysis before edits.

Exit-gate status:

- Satisfied for recording the current full-workspace failure evidence after
  aggregate hardening.
- Not satisfied for full workspace tests, full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor
  parity, release performance proof, default PlanExecutor switch, or goal
  completion.

## 2026-06-19 — Report Schema, Typecheck, and Runtime Workspace Failures Reduced

Status: resolved for the focused failures in `boon_report_schema`,
`boon_typecheck`, and `boon_runtime`. The full workspace gate remains open
because `boon_cli`, `boon_ir`, `boon_native_playground`, and `boon_parser`
still need current-state triage and verification.

Root causes fixed:

- `boon_report_schema`:
  `run_plan_root_scalar_scenario_accepts_bytes_length_expression_kind` had a
  stale forged legacy-delta fixture. The fixture set the legacy semantic delta
  to JSON number `4`, which is now the correct typed value after numeric delta
  replay was fixed. The forged case now uses an actually wrong numeric value,
  preserving typed numeric validation.
- `boon_typecheck`:
  `todomvc_type_hints_have_complete_store_and_source_shapes` selected the
  `store` function argument because richer source-shape inference made both
  `todo` and `store` argument hints contain `sources: [` in different contexts.
  The test now targets the `todo` row argument by refined row fields and
  asserts that input row shape remains separate from the returned row shape.
- `boon_runtime`:
  - Counter and derived cursor tests had stale JSON-string expectations for
    numeric state summaries. They now assert typed JSON numbers.
  - Todo row key-down sources were classified through the indexed-bool path,
    and that path reconstructed a new source event with key/text payloads
    cleared. The original routed source event is now preserved when applying
    indexed bool row actions, so shared key-down/blur sources can execute their
    text and bool branches together.
  - Enter title fallback logic only checked whether a title delta was emitted.
    Physical TodoMVC could emit a title delta with an empty/stale value and
    suppress the draft commit fallback. The fallback now checks whether the row
    title still differs from the non-empty edit draft before deciding the
    source action already handled the commit.
  - Cells compiled-artifact generic-derived runtime count now includes the
    BYTES formula scanner helper `formula_ascii_bytes`. The test now expects
    the current 12 functions and asserts that helper is present.

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_report_schema run_plan_root_scalar_scenario_accepts_bytes_length_expression_kind -- --nocapture` | Pass | Focused forged legacy-delta fixture test. |
| `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 cargo test -p boon_typecheck todomvc_type_hints_have_complete_store_and_source_shapes -- --nocapture` | Fail | Diagnostic run confirmed the panic was a test `unwrap` selecting the wrong argument hint. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck todomvc_type_hints_have_complete_store_and_source_shapes -- --nocapture` | Pass | Focused type-hint assertion after targeting the correct function argument. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime live_runtime_applies_numeric_counter_hold_updates_generically -- --nocapture` | Pass | Numeric state summary now asserted as JSON number. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_derived_cascade_dirties_unqualified_dependents_same_turn -- --nocapture` | Pass | Derived numeric cursor now asserted as JSON number. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime todo_title_updates_are_derived_from_ir_trim_branches -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed until row key-down source events preserved key/text payloads and Enter fallback committed stale/empty title cases from the edit draft. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime todo_editing_updates_are_derived_from_ir_bool_branches -- --nocapture` | Pass | Confirms Escape/editing branch still works with original source event preservation. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime live_runtime_routes_physical_todomvc_row_edit_events_by_target_text -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed until live runtime Enter fallback checked for stale/empty emitted title commits. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime compiled_artifact_decodes_cells_generic_derived_runtime_plan_without_ast -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Expected function count updated for legitimate `formula_ascii_bytes` BYTES helper. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- compile examples/cells.bn --out target/tmp/cells-debug.boonc --report target/reports/bytes-plan/debug/cells-compile-debug.json` | Pass | Diagnostic artifact confirmed current Cells source compiles with current `boon_cli` and BYTES formula helper. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- inspect-artifact target/tmp/cells-debug.boonc --report target/reports/bytes-plan/debug/cells-inspect-debug.json` | Pass | Diagnostic inspection reported `runtime_plan_generic_derived_deserialized_counts.function_count = 12`. |
| `jq '.runtime_plan.generic_derived.functions | length, [.[] | {name, arg_count: (.args|length), result: .result}]' target/tmp/cells-debug.boonc` | Pass | Confirmed `formula_ascii_bytes` is the additional decoded function. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime --lib --no-fail-fast` | Pass | `290 passed; 0 failed; finished in 963.02s`. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | Initial run required rustfmt wrapping changes; passed after `cargo fmt --all`. |
| `git diff --check` | Pass | No whitespace errors. |
| `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` | Pass | Existing warnings only. |
| `cargo test -p boon_report_schema --lib --no-fail-fast` | Pass | `28 passed; 0 failed`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck --lib --no-fail-fast` | Pass | `59 passed; 0 failed; finished in 133.71s`. |

Open findings after this follow-up:

- `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` has not been rerun after these
  fixes. The last full run failed seven targets; this follow-up proves three
  of those targets are now green in package-level runs.
- The remaining known full-workspace failing targets are expected to include
  `boon_cli`, `boon_ir`, `boon_native_playground`, and `boon_parser` until
  they are rerun and fixed or reclassified.
- The row source fixes improve current runtime correctness but do not complete
  full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor parity or Phase 10 default switch readiness.

Exit-gate status:

- Satisfied for the focused report-schema, typecheck, and runtime failures
  fixed above.
- Satisfied for full `boon_runtime`, `boon_report_schema`, and
  `boon_typecheck` package tests.
- Not satisfied for full workspace tests, full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor
  parity, release performance proof, default PlanExecutor switch, or goal
  completion.

## 2026-06-19 — Parser Root `items` List Memory Regression Fixed

Status: resolved for the parser target. The full workspace gate remains open
because `boon_cli`, `boon_ir`, and `boon_native_playground` still need current
triage and verification.

Independent audit recorded:

- Runtime/source-routing auditor:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee0ce-511e-72b3-8d0b-6da0c4be1342-runtime-source-routing-audit.json`
  - Confirmed typed numeric state summaries should remain JSON numbers.
  - Confirmed TodoMVC renamed row-source key-down and physical TodoMVC edit
    commits were real runtime routing/commit regressions, not expectation-only
    drift.
  - The main-agent runtime fixes are now covered by the full
    `boon_runtime` package test pass recorded above.

Root cause:

- Parser list-memory inference had a broad exclusion for fields named
  `items` and `children`, intended to avoid treating nested render-slot lists
  as app/source-facing list memories.
- That exclusion also hid legitimate root-level app state named `items` when
  written as `items: LIST {} |> List/map(...)`.
- The parser now distinguishes root app fields from nested render slots and
  directly records root `items`/`children` fields that contain an actual
  `LIST` literal. Unknown aliases such as `Widget/table` and
  `List/spreadsheet_rows` still do not create list memories.

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_parser/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee0ce-511e-72b3-8d0b-6da0c4be1342-runtime-source-routing-audit.json`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_parser --lib --no-fail-fast` | Fail, then Pass | Initially failed `list_unknown_alias_does_not_create_list_memories` and `widget_prefixed_symbols_do_not_create_list_memories`; after the root `items` list-memory fix, `47 passed; 0 failed`. |

Open findings after this follow-up:

- `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` has not yet been rerun after the
  parser/runtime/typecheck/report-schema fixes.
- Remaining known failing targets from the earlier broad run are expected to
  include `boon_cli`, `boon_ir`, and `boon_native_playground` until current
  package-level runs prove otherwise.

Exit-gate status:

- Satisfied for the parser target.
- Not satisfied for full workspace tests, full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor
  parity, release performance proof, default PlanExecutor switch, or goal
  completion.

## 2026-06-19 — CLI Default Scenario Resolver Uses Parser Fallback

Status: resolved for the CLI target. The full workspace gate remains open
because `boon_ir` and `boon_native_playground` still need current triage and
verification.

Root cause:

- `default_scenario` only matched examples by manifest source path or source
  filename.
- The test writes TodoMVC source to a temporary file with a deliberately
  misleading path. Manifest/filename matching must not infer the scenario from
  that path, but the current code also had no parser-backed fallback.

What changed:

- `default_scenario` now loads manifest entries once, checks exact
  path/filename matches first, then parses the source file and maps known
  structural example identity to the manifest entry.
- The parser fallback currently recognizes TodoMVC by a `todos` list memory
  and Cells by `cells_app` or a `cells` list memory. Unknown files still return
  the explicit “add it to manifest or pass --scenario” error.

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | Initial parser/CLI formatting required rustfmt; passed after `cargo fmt --all`. |
| `git diff --check` | Pass | No whitespace errors. |

## 2026-06-19 - IR Source Wrapper and Typed Value Lowering Failures Fixed

Status: resolved for the `boon_ir` target and rechecked for the affected
`boon_typecheck` target. The full workspace gate remains open because
`boon_native_playground` and any newly exposed workspace failures still need a
fresh broad run.

Independent audit recorded:

- IR/typechecker wrapper auditor:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee0ff-7b95-78a0-b0be-49a33a174231-boon-ir-wrapper-audit.json`
  - Classified the `MatchConst` vs `MatchValueConst` failures as stale test
    expectations after typed value lowering.
  - Classified the `SOURCE` wrapper failures as real engine/typechecker bugs,
    not Boon-level workaround candidates.
  - Confirmed the TodoMVC source payload and NovyWave source-wrapper cases were
    green after the local fixes.
  - Left a medium finding that `MatchConst`/`MatchValueConst` still need
    explicit MachinePlan support before bounded executable-plan completion.

Root causes fixed:

- Typed value lowering now intentionally represents structured value-match arm
  outputs with `UpdateExpression::MatchValueConst`. The stale IR tests now
  assert the typed value expression shape instead of the older scalar-only
  `MatchConst` form.
- `SOURCE` statements wrapping render constructor expressions lost the
  constructor result kind in static type inference. The typechecker now honors
  the attached expression type for `SOURCE` statements before falling back to
  source-payload typing.
- `THEN { SOURCE ... }` continuations inside static wrapper pipelines could
  collapse the previous render type. Static statement pipeline typing now treats
  source-pipe continuations as preserving the preceding render value.
- Cross-root render constructor lookups were too strict for module-prefixed
  constructors. `RenderContractRegistry::constructor_shape` now falls back to
  the root contract that registered the constructor when the active root lacks
  it.
- NovyWave source-wrapped controls exposed a derived `List/latest` same-tick
  dependency passthrough. IR lowering now uses the dependency source branch and
  guard when a derived dependency branch directly passes through a
  `List/latest` result, instead of reading the stale derived field.
- Source address inference accepted zero-score row candidates, which could make
  non-address fields such as TodoMVC checkbox `completed` look like source
  payload fields. Zero-score candidates are now rejected.

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee0ff-7b95-78a0-b0be-49a33a174231-boon-ir-wrapper-audit.json`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_ir --lib --no-fail-fast` | Fail, then Pass | Initially exposed seven current IR failures. After fixes, `61 passed; 0 failed; finished in 138.66s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_ir --lib wrapper_binds_nested_element_events_generically -- --nocapture` | Pass | Focused wrapper rerun covered both wrapper tests through the shared filter; `2 passed; 0 failed`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck --lib --no-fail-fast` | Pass | Rechecked affected typechecker behavior; `59 passed; 0 failed; finished in 133.31s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_ir --lib derived_local_event... nested_then... ...` | Fail | Diagnostic command shape was wrong because Cargo accepts only one test filter. Superseded by the full `boon_ir` package run. |
| `cargo test -p boon_ir --lib source_wrapper_binds_nested_element_events_generically source_continuation...` | Fail | Diagnostic command shape was wrong because Cargo accepts only one test filter. Superseded by the shared wrapper filter and full package run. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck --lib source_pipe -- --nocapture` | Pass | Filter matched zero tests; treated only as a diagnostic non-signal. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | Initial run required rustfmt after source edits; passed after `cargo fmt --all`. |
| `git diff --check` | Pass | No whitespace errors. |

Open findings after this follow-up:

- `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` and
  `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` have not yet been rerun after the
  latest IR/typechecker fixes.
- `boon_native_playground` remains the most likely package-level red target
  from previous broad evidence until a fresh run proves the current state.
- The `MatchConst`/`MatchValueConst` MachinePlan execution support is still a
  real Phase 5/6 risk and cannot be considered complete from IR tests alone.
- Full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor parity, release performance proof, default
  PlanExecutor switch, and goal completion remain unsatisfied.

Exit-gate status:

- Satisfied for the focused `boon_ir` and affected `boon_typecheck` failures.
- Not satisfied for full workspace tests, full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor
  parity, release performance proof, default PlanExecutor switch, or goal
  completion.

Initial classification:

- The highest-leverage cluster is native source-intent/hit metadata and row
  binding resolution:
  - Cells cannot resolve `store.selected_input.sources.editor.select`.
  - TodoMVC edit input cannot find
    `todo.sources.editing_todo_title_element.change` by target text.

    `address` intents but not the target-associated source intent expected by
    the native tests.

    identity for `signal_catalog`.
- A second cluster is hover/focus state not being marked for native host hover:
  TodoMVC filter/theme/clear-completed hover assertions see `None` instead of
  `Some(Bool(true))`.
- The core runtime source route layer is not the primary suspect, because the
  same workspace run passed overlapping runtime tests for observed Cells source
  events, physical TodoMVC source events, NovyWave file rows, NovyWave selected
  row formatter, and generic source routing.

- The native failures still block the broad workspace test gate and must be
  fixed or precisely classified before any completion claim.
- Focused native reruns are needed next, starting with the source-intent
  failure around `store.selected_input.sources.editor.select`, because it
  appears in multiple Cells and typed-route tests.
- Full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor parity, release performance proof, default

## 2026-06-19 - Native Source Canonicalization And TodoMVC Hover Helper Fixes

Status: partial native playground recovery. The full native target improved
from `150 passed; 22 failed` to `155 passed; 17 failed`. The TodoMVC hover
cluster and the typed route table test are now green in the full target, but
native source binding, projected-row identity, dev/editor UI state, and some
Cells scroll/edit tests still fail.

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_native_playground/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee139-54d8-7872-911d-deaddbe133bc-native-source-intent-hover-audit.json`

Decisions:

- Native document source canonicalization now distinguishes known source paths
  and local row aliases from dynamic root state paths. This lets root data such
  as `store.selected_input.sources.editor.select` resolve to the current
  concrete Cells editor source while preserving row-local NovyWave aliases such
  as `file_tree_row.file_row_elements.select_file`.
- Native source nodes may carry both `address` and `target` intents. Target
  inference no longer skips a row only because an address intent already exists.
- Helper-level hover tests must follow the real preview pipeline:
  `preview_update_hover_from_input` updates input state,
  `preview_apply_hover_overlay` marks overlay state dirty, and
  `latest_preview_render_frame` materializes `__hover` / `__hover_paint` on a
  render-frame clone. Tests should not expect `preview_update_hover_from_input`
  to mutate `layout_frame_override` directly.
- For row-hover delete reveal, missing `__hover` is equivalent to direct hover
  false when the node has no base direct-hover style. The test now asserts the
  revealed delete button is not `__hover=true` while still requiring
  `__hover_paint=true`.

| `cargo test -p boon_native_playground --bin boon_native_playground novywave_external_loaded_file_tree_renders_loaded_file_without_workspace_rows -- --nocapture` | Pass | Focused NovyWave file-row route check after preserving local row aliases; `1 passed; 0 failed; finished in 44.30s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_native_playground --bin boon_native_playground preview_hover_and_click_use_typed_route_table_without_proof_hit_json -- --nocapture` | Pass | Focused typed route table check; `1 passed; 0 failed; finished in 44.59s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_native_playground --bin boon_native_playground todomvc_layout_uses_generic_visual_contracts -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | First rerun exposed stale raw-frame hover assertions; after switching to rendered-frame overlay checks, `1 passed; 0 failed; finished in 0.38s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_native_playground --bin boon_native_playground physical_todomvc_footer_links_and_theme_buttons_fit_inline -- --nocapture` | Pass | `1 passed; 0 failed; finished in 149.01s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_native_playground --bin boon_native_playground physical_todomvc_switcher_hover_glass_and_checkbox_readbacks_are_visual -- --nocapture` | Pass | `1 passed; 0 failed; finished in 157.70s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_native_playground --bin boon_native_playground --no-fail-fast` | Fail | Improved to `155 passed; 17 failed; finished in 758.33s`. TodoMVC hover tests and typed route table test passed inside the full target. |
| `git diff --check` | Pass | No whitespace errors after the native patches. |

## 2026-06-19 - Cells Escape Cancel Root Cause Fixed

Status: focused runtime/native Cells cancel recovery. The earlier native
failure `cells_escape_cancels_draft_without_saving` was not a WGPU overlay,
summary-cache, or Boon example issue. A new pure runtime regression reproduced
the same stale `editing_text` state: a key-only Escape cancel event skipped the
indexed text `ReadPath` branch for `editing_text`, so only `editing = false`
was applied. Removing that invalid Escape skip lets
`sources.editor.cancel |> THEN { formula_text }` run correctly.

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_native_playground/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee159-e38e-7402-8500-f98bf9e76cfb-cells-todomvc-source-binding-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee15a-2655-7781-90be-46266da71271-novywave-row-workflow-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee16e-ebd9-7462-986e-de43836a4077-native-cells-cancel-audit.json`

Root cause:

- Runtime `SourceRouteTextAction::ReadPath` for indexed text fields skipped
  when `input.key == Some("Escape")` and no text payload was present.
- Native Escape sends a key-only cancel event. For Cells this must still run
  the row-local `ReadPath` branch for `cell.editing_text`, because
  `examples/cells/cell.bn` defines cancel as
  `sources.editor.cancel |> THEN { formula_text }`.
- The skip left the uncommitted draft `"42"` in `editing_text`, while
  `formula_text` and `value` stayed at `"5"` and the bool branch still set
  `editing = false`.

Decisions:

- Key-only Escape is a valid trigger for `ReadPath` update branches. Payload
  text still suppresses `ReadPath` branches to avoid mixing text-payload edits
  with rollback/copy-from-current-row branches.
- Added a pure runtime regression so future failures do not require the native
  WGPU/playground path to diagnose.
- Added `"escape"` to native `source_intent_kind_routes_event` so Escape
  source intents participate in occurrence/address proofing consistently with
  click/change/blur/key-down intents.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | New regression first reproduced stale `editing_text = "42"` after cancel. After removing the Escape skip, `1 passed; 0 failed; finished in 48.80s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_native_playground --bin boon_native_playground cells_escape_cancels_draft_without_saving -- --nocapture` | Pass | Original native regression passed after the runtime fix; first rerun finished in `49.60s`, second rerun after the native Escape classifier cleanup finished in `49.95s`. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | Initial check exposed formatting needed in the native source canonicalization helper. Passed after `cargo fmt --all`. |
| `cargo fmt --all` | Pass | Applied rustfmt to the current worktree. |
| `git diff --check` | Pass | No whitespace errors after formatting. |

Subagent evidence:

- Saved
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee159-e38e-7402-8500-f98bf9e76cfb-cells-todomvc-source-binding-audit.json`.
  The audit identified the original Cells selected-input source canonicalization
  bug and the TodoMVC stale-source metadata risk.
- Saved
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee15a-2655-7781-90be-46266da71271-novywave-row-workflow-audit.json`.
  The audit identified open NovyWave row-list identity, expand/select workflow,
  indent, and stale formatter-test issues.
- Saved
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee16e-ebd9-7462-986e-de43836a4077-native-cells-cancel-audit.json`.
  The audit independently ruled out stale native overlay/summary state and
  confirmed the failure pointed back to runtime source-action coverage.

Current native status after focused fixes:

- Focused now-passing tests include
  `cells_formula_bar_click_accepts_text_edit`,
  `cells_clicking_another_cell_blurs_and_saves_current_draft`, and
  `cells_escape_cancels_draft_without_saving`.
- The full native target has not been rerun after this cancel fix, so the
  previous `155 passed; 17 failed` full-target result is stale for these three
  Cells tests and still authoritative only for the broad unresolved-cluster
  picture.

Open findings after this follow-up:

- A runtime-focused Cells cancel subagent audit was started but did not complete
  before it stopped being useful; it was closed without a report after the local
  regression and the native audit independently proved the root-cause area.
- TodoMVC post-edit source metadata, NovyWave selected-row/list identity,
  NovyWave expand/select workflow, dev/editor UI failures, Cells scroll tests,
  `physical_todomvc_toggle_all_completes_then_clears_every_row`, full
  workspace tests, full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor parity, release performance
  proof, and the default PlanExecutor switch remain unsatisfied.

Exit-gate status:

- Satisfied for the focused runtime and native Cells Escape cancel regression.
- Not satisfied for the native playground target, full workspace tests, full
  TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor parity, release performance proof, default
  PlanExecutor switch, or goal completion.

  `cells_clicking_another_cell_blurs_and_saves_current_draft`,

  instead of `selected_signal.format_elements.*`, and the simple expanded scope

  PlanExecutor switch, or goal completion.

### Physical TodoMVC row edit source-route guard fix

Recorded at: `2026-06-19`

Status: focused Phase 6 runtime/source-route correctness improved, but the
phase remains partial.

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Root cause found:

- The physical TodoMVC row edit flow exposed two generic source-route bugs in
  aggregate row sources such as `todo.todo_elements.editing_todo_title_element`.
- The compiler lowered guarded dependency updates as unguarded branches. In
  particular, `title_to_update |> THEN { Text/empty() }` and direct dependency
  passthroughs like `title <- title_to_update` did not inherit the dependency's
  `key_down.key |> WHEN { Enter => ..., __ => SKIP }` guard.
- Guard extraction also missed valid multi-line pipeline syntax because the
  payload path and the `WHEN` expression can occupy adjacent AST lines, and
  `Enter => []` did not always produce `MatchArm` AST nodes. The IR now has a
  generic inline-symbol fallback for accepted non-`SKIP` match patterns.
- Runtime source-text actions could still execute for aggregate source events
  that had no text/named text payload. This let key-only events participate in
  text-update actions. The runtime now skips `SourceText` indexed text actions
  when the source event carries neither `text` nor named textual payload.

Behavioral evidence:

- Before the final compiler guard fix, diagnostic output showed `cancel-edit`
  changed `todos[4].title` from `"Ship physical TodoMVC"` to `""`, so
  reopening the editor copied an empty title into `edited_title`.
- After the fix, `cancel-edit` emits only `store.selected_todo_id = "None"` for
  the focused row and leaves `title`, `edited_title`, and `draft_title` intact.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_ir physical_todomvc_mode_toggle_lowers_to_match_const_expression -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed repeatedly while the IR branch for `todo.edited_title` and then `todo.title` had `guard: None`. Final clean pass: `1 passed; finished in 144.93s`; expanded assertion pass after direct dependency guard propagation: `1 passed; finished in 148.36s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime live_runtime_routes_physical_todomvc_row_edit_events_by_target_text -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed first with `todos[4].edited_title == ""` after reopen. Diagnostic run proved Escape had cleared `title`; after guard propagation and source-text payload skip, diagnostic pass finished in `149.87s`. Final clean pass: `1 passed; finished in 161.68s`. |

Current findings:

- The focused physical TodoMVC row-edit route now preserves title/draft state
  across Escape and reopen in the public runtime.
- The physical TodoMVC feedback loop is still too slow for efficient debugging:
  the focused IR/runtime tests each take roughly 145-162 seconds. This is
  measurement evidence for the Phase 6/9 slow-path investigation, not a
  completed performance fix.
- Full physical TodoMVC native/operator parity remains open; this focused
  runtime pass does not prove the native playground target or PlanExecutor
  default switch.

### Physical TodoMVC toggle-all live runtime and PlanExecutor parity fix

Recorded at: `2026-06-20`

Status: focused Phase 6 runtime/native/PlanExecutor parity improved, but the
phase remains partial.

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Root causes found:

- The native/physical toggle-all failure was not a renderer shortcut problem.
  `store.elements.toggle_all_checkbox` reached the live runtime, but
  `GenericScheduledRuntime::derived_bool_value` returned a materialized
  `store.all_completed` bool from root storage before recomputing the
  count-derived all-complete value. The second click therefore read stale
  `all_completed=false` and wrote `completed=true` to every row again.
- The PlanExecutor toggle-all path had the correct final row state after the
  runtime fix, but still failed legacy semantic-delta parity. Initial row-local
  Bool/not fields such as `not_completed` were not materialized before the first
  action, unchanged bulk source writes were skipped for unscoped list updates,
  and bulk deltas were emitted row-by-row instead of legacy source-first then
  derived-second order.
- The report-schema replay had the same older single-row assumptions. It now
  mirrors bulk unscoped indexed target expansion, count-derived
  `all_completed`, initial row-local Bool/not materialization, source delta
  emission for every bulk-touched row, and source-first/derived-second bulk
  ordering.

Behavioral evidence:

- The focused runtime regression now clicks
  `store.elements.toggle_all_checkbox` twice in
  `examples/todo_mvc_physical/RUN.bn`. After the first click every row is
  completed; after the second click every row is active again and
  `store.all_completed=false`.
- The native focused regression
  `physical_todomvc_toggle_all_completes_then_clears_every_row` now passes
  through the native playground route.
- The focused PlanExecutor report
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/debug-todomvc-toggle-all-run-plan.json` is
  `status=pass`, has `legacy_comparison.passed=true`,
  `state_match=true`, `semantic_delta_match=true`, and ends with
  `active_count=4`, `completed_count=0`.
- The same report records no fallback counters for the proven slice:
  `runtime_ast_eval_count=0`, `executable_string_path_count=0`,
  `unknown_plan_op_count=0`, `graph_rebuild_count=0`, and
  `graph_clones_per_item=0`.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime live_runtime_physical_todomvc_toggle_all_recomputes_all_completed_between_clicks -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed before the stale-derived-bool fix with every row still completed after the second click. Final pass: `1 passed; finished in 165.87s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_native_playground --bin boon_native_playground physical_todomvc_toggle_all_completes_then_clears_every_row -- --nocapture` | Pass | Proves the focused native playground toggle-all route after the runtime fix. Final pass: `1 passed; finished in 150.02s`. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- run-plan-root-scalar-scenario examples/todomvc.bn --scenario examples/todomvc.scn --steps toggle-all-complete,toggle-all-active --compare-legacy --report target/reports/bytes-plan/debug-todomvc-toggle-all-run-plan.json` | Fail, then Pass | Failed first on semantic-delta parity, while final state already matched. After PlanExecutor parity fixes the report passes and matches legacy semantic deltas. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/debug-todomvc-toggle-all-run-plan.json` | Fail, then Pass | Failed first on single-row replay assumptions and missing typed state input for the bulk Bool/not branch. After schema replay updates it passes. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting check after the focused patches. |
| `cargo check -p boon_report_schema -p boon_runtime -p boon_cli -p xtask --quiet` | Pass | Existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings remain. |
| `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` | Pass | Finished in `1m 02s`; existing native GPU / native playground dead-code warnings remain. |
| `cargo xtask audit-goal-readiness --report target/reports/bytes-plan/goal-readiness.json` | Removed verifier at the time | xtask reported this legacy Ply/COSMIC command had been removed from active verification and pointed to native GPU gates. This historical result is superseded by the active BYTES/MachinePlan readiness preflight added later. |
| `git diff --check` | Pass | No whitespace errors in the current diff. |

Open findings:

- This closes one focused toggle-all correctness/parity slice. It does not close
  Phase 6, the full native playground target, full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor
  parity, default PlanExecutor switching, release performance proof, or the
  whole BYTES/MachinePlan goal.
- The focused tests are still very slow: roughly 150-166 seconds for a single
  runtime/native case. That remains an active performance problem, not a solved
  one.
- The focused report is a debug proof. It still needs to be folded into the
  aggregate BYTES/MachinePlan verification path before the roadmap can claim a
  complete acceptance gate for TodoMVC toggle-all.

### Indexed BYTES source payload parity and aggregate gate refresh

Recorded at: `2026-06-20`

Status: Phase 6/7/9 proof surface improved. The current BYTES/MachinePlan
aggregate gate passes for the existing 37 required child reports, but the
overall goal remains partial because the default PlanExecutor switch, full
workspace tests, broad performance proof, and final independent audit set are
not complete.

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Root causes found:

- The one remaining aggregate child failure was
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-indexed-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`.
  The CPU PlanExecutor correctly resolved `row.receive` / `row.inspect` by
  hidden `target_key=2`, `target_generation=1` and emitted
  `FieldSet:payload`, `FieldSet:payload_len`, and `FieldSet:payload_second`.
- The legacy runtime skipped indexed `SourceText` actions when `input.text` was
  absent and the textual payload map was empty. BYTES source payloads populate
  `input.payload_bytes`, not the textual payload map, so `receive.bytes` was
  skipped before `commit_indexed_bytes_source` could run.
- The legacy row-bound resolver also only read hidden row identity from
  `user_action`; it now accepts hidden `target_key` / `target_generation` from
  the typed source event as well. This aligns legacy comparison with
  PlanExecutor without exposing keys to Boon source.
- The standalone report-schema aggregate contract still expected the older
  child-report list and did not require the new TodoMVC toggle-all report. It
  now requires `todomvc-toggle-all`, matching the xtask aggregate.

Subagent evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/2026-06-20-indexed-bytes-source-route-audit.md`
  independently identified the BYTES payload early-skip guard as the likely
  culprit and recommended the focused regression command.

Behavioral evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-indexed-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
  is now `status=pass`, with
  `legacy_comparison.passed=true`, `state_match=true`, and
  `semantic_delta_match=true`.
- The indexed BYTES report now records semantic delta signatures
  `FieldSet:payload`, `FieldSet:payload_len`, and `FieldSet:payload_second`.
- The same report records no fallback counters for the proven slice:
  `runtime_ast_eval_count=0`, `executable_string_path_count=0`,
  `unknown_plan_op_count=0`, `graph_rebuild_count=0`, and
  `graph_clones_per_item=0`.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` is now
  `status=pass`, `aggregate_checks_pass=true`,
  `required_report_count=37`, `checked_report_count=37`,
  `proof_report_count=30`, `diagnostic_report_count=7`, and has no blockers.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_scalar_plan_executor_replays_indexed_bytes_source_payload_update -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed before the BYTES payload skip fix; final pass: `1 passed; finished in 7.83s`. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- run-plan-root-scalar-scenario examples/bytes_indexed_source_payload_plan_ops.bn --scenario examples/bytes_indexed_source_payload_plan_ops.scn --steps receive-beta-bytes,inspect-beta-bytes --compare-legacy --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-indexed-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json` | Fail, then Pass | Failed first with missing legacy BYTES payload and `Bytes/get` deltas. Final report passes and matches legacy semantic deltas. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-indexed-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json` | Pass | Focused indexed BYTES report schema check after the runtime fix. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Fail, then Pass | Failed first on the indexed BYTES report, then on stale child reports after binary/schema rebuilds. After refreshing required children, final aggregate passes with 37/37 reports checked. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Fail, then Pass | Failed until report-schema expected children included `todomvc-toggle-all`. Final standalone aggregate schema check passes. |
| `cargo fmt --all` | Pass | Applied rustfmt after resolver/schema edits. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting check after the final edits. |
| `cargo check -p boon_report_schema -p boon_runtime -p boon_cli -p xtask --quiet` | Pass | Existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings remain. |
| `git diff --check` | Pass | No whitespace errors in the current diff. |

Open findings:

- This removes the current aggregate BYTES/MachinePlan blocker for the existing
  required child reports. It does not finish Phase 10 or prove that the
  PlanExecutor can become the default engine.
- The full workspace test suite and full native playground target remain
  outside this slice.
- The focused runtime/PlanExecutor tests are still slow enough to be a
  performance concern; this slice fixed a correctness/parity bug, not the
  underlying slow feedback loop.

### Full workspace gate interruption after runtime hot loop

Status: The final broad `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` gate was
started after the aggregate BYTES/MachinePlan report passed, but it was
interrupted manually because the `boon_runtime` test binary stopped producing
log output while using roughly five CPU cores. Treat this as neither a pass nor
a normal deterministic fail. It is a live performance/reliability blocker that
needs a focused follow-up instead of letting the full workspace gate burn CPU
blindly.

Evidence:

- Wrapper PID `3126840` ran
  `cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast` and wrote
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/final-cargo-test-workspace.log`.
- Runtime test PID `3277702`
  (`target/debug/deps/boon_runtime-3d26acd1ff164d5e`) was observed at about
  `491%` CPU after `14m36s` elapsed inside the runtime package.
- A 12-second progress check showed the log timestamp and size unchanged:
  `1781915686 213537` before and after the wait.
- The last completed runtime log line was
  `test tests::source_initializers_are_read_from_boon_text ... ok`.
- Earlier in the same runtime package,
  `tests::top_level_source_updates_indexed_bool_rows_with_row_context`
  had already failed.
- A stack sample was attempted with `gdb`, but the local ptrace policy rejected
  the attach. The failed attach output is saved at
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/final-cargo-test-workspace-hang-gdb.txt`.
- The process tree was stopped with `TERM` for only the matching workspace-test
  wrapper/cargo/runtime-test process IDs.

Known full-workspace partial failures before interruption:

- `boon_native_playground` had already reported failures including
  `editor_primary_clipboard_shortcuts_work_with_native_key_names`,
  `novywave_files_scopes_variables_manual_workflow_is_deterministic`,
  `novywave_search_and_waveform_keyboard_work_from_real_preview_input`,
  `novywave_row_formatter_real_click_opens_and_rerenders_preview_layout`,
  `novywave_operator_host_input_batches_execute_in_preview_runtime`, and
  `novywave_controls_lower_hover_material_and_pointer_contracts`.
- `boon_runtime` had at least the failing
  `top_level_source_updates_indexed_bool_rows_with_row_context` test before the
  hot/stalled section.

Next action:

- Do not rerun the full workspace gate blindly until the runtime hot/stall is
  isolated. Run a focused `boon_runtime --lib --no-fail-fast` or filtered
  runtime test under a timeout first, identify the test after
  `source_initializers_are_read_from_boon_text`, and add either a real engine
  fix or an explicit timeout/profiling report.
- The aggregate BYTES/MachinePlan pass remains valid for the existing child
  reports, but the full goal remains incomplete until this runtime hot/stall
  and the native playground failures are resolved.

Focused follow-up:

- The incomplete runtime-test comparison identified four physical TodoMVC
  runtime tests that had not printed a final result before the full workspace
  gate was stopped:
  `physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes`,
  `physical_todomvc_add_todo_defaults_active_and_remains_toggleable`,
  `physical_todomvc_router_filter_source_updates_selected_filter`, and
  `physical_todomvc_theme_source_event_updates_latest_root_text`.
- A focused rerun of
  `cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`
  reproduced the CPU problem immediately. PID `3403473` used about one full
  core for 40 seconds, emitted only the harness line
  `test tests::physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes ...`,
  and was stopped manually before the 240-second timeout to avoid unnecessary
  CPU load.
- A zombie check found one unrelated defunct `sd_espeak-ng-mb` process at
  `0.0%` CPU. The CPU load was not a zombie; it was the focused Boon runtime
  test process.
- Focused log:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes.log`.

Updated next action:

- Inspect the physical TodoMVC runtime test/setup path before more heavy runs.
  The next execution must include early internal phase checkpoints or a short
  timeout so the exact hot phase can be identified without another long CPU
  burn.

### Physical TodoMVC runtime lowering hot path isolated

Recorded at: `2026-06-20`

Status: The immediate runtime CPU hot/stall from the interrupted full workspace
gate is now understood and partially fixed. The focused physical TodoMVC
runtime tests no longer burn indefinitely, but the full workspace gate has not
yet been rerun and the native playground failures remain open.

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Root cause:

- The hot process was not a zombie. It was the focused runtime test
  `physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes`.
- Phase probes showed `parse_project` completed in roughly `0.53s`, then
  `boon_ir::lower` entered `boon_typecheck::check_profiled`.
- Runtime lowering still requested full developer/editor typecheck work:
  top-level function-body diagnostics, `function_type_table` construction, and
  `ensure_all_expressions` over every expression in the large physical TodoMVC
  scene/theme program.
- Before the fix, the typechecker spent tens of seconds expanding physical
  TodoMVC theme/render helper functions such as `Theme/*`, `todo_title_text`,
  `delete_button_text`, `filter_button_text`, `todo_title_element`, and
  `remove_todo_button`. A probe run showed `ensure_all_expressions` alone
  taking about `85.177s`.
- This work is useful for full compiler/editor diagnostics, but it is the wrong
  default for runtime execution tests. Runtime lowering only needs runtime
  source/state/list/render metadata, not full type-hint table coverage for
  every helper function.

Fix:

- `boon_typecheck::check_runtime_profiled` now skips the two developer-diagnostic
  surfaces that caused the hot path:
  top-level function body sweeps and the full `ensure_all_expressions` pass.
  Full `check_profiled` still performs both, so compiler/editor diagnostics are
  not silently removed.
- `boon_runtime` now uses a local `lower_for_runtime` helper backed by
  `boon_ir::lower_runtime_profiled` for runtime scenario paths and physical
  TodoMVC runtime harnesses. Debug/dump/full compiler paths can still use full
  `lower()`.

Evidence:

- Probe log before the final fix:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes-typehint-skip.log`.
  It passed but spent about `85.211s` in typecheck and `87.57s` in the focused
  test body after compilation.
- Clean focused log after removing probes:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes-clean.log`.
  It passed with `1 passed; finished in 2.21s` after a one-time `32.54s`
  recompile.
- Physical TodoMVC runtime filter:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-physical_todomvc-filter-clean.log`.
  It passed `9 passed; 0 failed; finished in 13.53s`.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeout 20s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 BOON_PROBE_PHYSICAL_TODOMVC=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Timeout | Proved parse completed in about `535ms` and the test was hot during lowering. |
| `timeout 18s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 BOON_PROBE_PHYSICAL_TODOMVC=1 BOON_PROBE_IR_LOWER=1 BOON_PROBE_TYPECHECK=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Timeout | Proved typechecker initialization was cheap and the hot phase was `check_statements`. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 BOON_PROBE_PHYSICAL_TODOMVC=1 BOON_PROBE_IR_LOWER=1 BOON_PROBE_TYPECHECK=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | After skipping runtime function-body diagnostics, still showed `ensure_all_expressions` at about `85.177s`; test body `87.57s`. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 BOON_PROBE_PHYSICAL_TODOMVC=1 BOON_PROBE_IR_LOWER=1 BOON_PROBE_TYPECHECK=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | After skipping runtime full-expression coverage, lowering finished in about `2.007s`; test body `2.06s`. |
| `timeout 90s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Clean run after removing probes: compile `32.54s`, test body `2.21s`. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_ -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | `9 passed; 0 failed; finished in 13.53s`. |
| `cargo fmt --all` | Pass | Applied formatting after runtime/typechecker edits. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting check after removing probe hooks and the unused import. |
| `env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo check -p boon_typecheck -p boon_runtime --quiet` | Pass | Targeted compile check for the edited typechecker/runtime crates. |

Open findings:

- This fixes the focused physical TodoMVC runtime CPU hot path, but it does not
  prove full workspace readiness.
- Full compiler/editor typecheck for the physical TodoMVC program can still be
  expensive because it intentionally performs full type-hint coverage. That is a
  separate compiler performance target, not the immediate runtime hot loop.
- The previous full workspace native playground failures remain unresolved.

### Indexed row source guard correctness fix

Recorded at: `2026-06-20`

Status: Fixed a deterministic runtime correctness failure discovered after the
physical TodoMVC hot-path work. This is an engine/compiler fix, not a Boon
source workaround.

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee28d-5acf-7031-a21d-8a652e213d10-indexed-row-source-guard-audit.md`

Root cause:

- The focused test
  `top_level_source_updates_indexed_bool_rows_with_row_context` failed after
  `clear_all`: pressing top-level `store.elements.select_clk` with
  `target_text = "clk"` left `signals[0].selected` as `false`.
- Temporary probes showed direct row resolution was correct:
  `source=store.elements.select_clk list=Some("signals") index=Some(0)
  target_text=Some("clk")`.
- The indexed bool action was skipped before evaluation because IR guard
  inference converted the structural pulse
  `store.elements.select_clk.event.press` into
  `UpdateGuard::SourcePayloadOneOf { field: Named("press"), values:
  ["False", "True"] }`.
- Normal SOURCE press events do not carry a named `press` payload. The guard was
  therefore false even though the SOURCE event itself was present.

Fix:

- Added guard-specific source-payload matching in `boon_ir`.
- General source matching still recognizes bare event pulses such as
  `event.press` so source expression lowering continues to work.
- Guard inference now ignores bare `event.<name>` / `events.<name>` pulses and
  only creates payload guards for actual payload-bearing forms such as
  `change.text`, `event.change.bytes`, and `event.key_down.key`.
- Added `event_press_pulse_is_not_payload_guard_field` to lock this distinction.
- Kept the runtime row-context regression
  `top_level_source_updates_indexed_bool_rows_with_row_context` as the accepted
  surface proof.

Subagent evidence:

- Explorer `019ee28d-5acf-7031-a21d-8a652e213d10` independently confirmed row
  lookup was not the problem and identified the fake `press` payload guard as
  the likely culprit.
- Saved report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee28d-5acf-7031-a21d-8a652e213d10-indexed-row-source-guard-audit.md`.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeout 60s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime top_level_source_updates_indexed_bool_rows_with_row_context -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Fail | Reproduced the runtime row-context failure before the fix. |
| `timeout 60s env BOON_DEBUG_ROW_SOURCE=1 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime top_level_source_updates_indexed_bool_rows_with_row_context -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Fail | Temporary probes proved row index `0` was resolved and the indexed bool action was skipped by a fake `press` payload guard. Probes were removed. |
| `timeout 90s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_ir source_payload -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | `4 passed`; existing source-payload guard behavior still works. |
| `timeout 60s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_ir event_press_pulse_is_not_payload_guard_field -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | New focused helper regression for event-pulse vs payload-guard distinction. |
| `timeout 60s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime top_level_source_updates_indexed_bool_rows_with_row_context -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Fixed runtime row-context behavior. |
| `timeout 60s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime row_scoped_source_without_row_context_does_not_bulk_apply_indexed_bool -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Existing row-scope safety regression still passes. |
| `cargo fmt --all && cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting applied and verified. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo check -p boon_ir -p boon_runtime --quiet` | Pass | Touched crates compile. |

Open findings:

- This fixes one deterministic runtime correctness failure from the interrupted
  broader gate. It does not complete full Phase 6 parity or the full workspace
  gate.
- The source-payload/event distinction should later be represented more
  explicitly in typed MachinePlan source payload schemas so plan guards cannot
  be inferred from ambiguous path suffixes.

### Cells runtime initialization slow path isolated and reduced

Recorded at: `2026-06-20`

Status: Partially fixed. The focused Cells cancel test no longer spends roughly
45 seconds in startup, but the remaining reset-source prepass still costs about
5.5 seconds in debug test builds. This is improved enough to stop the current
debugging loop and move to other BYTES/MachinePlan tasks, but it is not the
final Cells startup architecture.

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee2a2-e8ab-7f81-a7d2-0f35d0239671-cells-runtime-init-audit.md`

Root cause:

- The focused test
  `cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text` was
  slow in `LiveRuntime::from_source`, not in event handling.
- Probe evidence before the fix showed parse/lower/verify/compile at about
  `154ms`, while `LoadedRuntime::new` took about `44.5s`.
- The existing runtime profile identified
  `GenericScheduledRuntime::from_compiled_profiled` as the owner:
  `initialize_generic_derived_first_ms` was about `44.9s`,
  `indexed_key_count=10452`, and `indexed_recompute_ms` was about `40.5s`.
- Field-level profiling showed the cold startup cost was mostly eager
  full-grid pure indexed derivation:
  `cells.value` was about `33.7s`, `cells.address` about `6.3s`, and
  `cells.default_formula` about `0.24s`.
- The old initialization sequence computed all indexed derived fields before
  `reset_indexed_holds_from_storage_plan`, then reset indexed HOLD fields, then
  computed all indexed derived fields again. For Cells this meant evaluating
  every row `value` while row HOLDs such as `formula_text` were not yet seeded.

Fix:

- Replaced the first full indexed-derived initialization pass with a targeted
  reset-source prepass.
- The prepass computes the ordered indexed derived prefix needed by
  `RuntimeStorageIndexedRowInitialReset` source fields. For Cells this computes
  `cells.address` and `cells.default_formula` so row HOLD reset can seed
  `formula_text` and `editing_text`.
- After reset, startup no longer eagerly initializes pure indexed derived
  fields. Pure row values are left to the existing on-demand
  `read_list_field -> recompute_generic_derived_key_value` path.
- The normal post-reset initialization still materializes root derived fields
  and any non-pure indexed derived startup keys.

Evidence:

- Before fix, focused probe log:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-cells-cancel-init-field-profile.log`.
  The test passed but finished in `46.64s`; runtime init was about
  `45.94s`; first indexed derived initialization was about `44.96s`; cold
  `cells.value` alone was about `33.74s`.
- Intermediate reset-source-only experiment:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-cells-cancel-reset-source-prepass.log`.
  This proved computing only `default_formula` was insufficient because it
  recursively recomputed `address` per row.
- Intermediate dependency-prefix experiment:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-cells-cancel-reset-source-prefix.log`.
  This moved the startup cost but still left eager `cells.value` at about
  `31.25s`.
- Final focused log:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-cells-lazy-indexed-focused.log`.
  The cancel test passed with `1 passed; finished in 7.77s` after a one-time
  recompile. Related Cells dependency tests also passed.
- Final probe log before removing probes:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/runtime-cells-cancel-lazy-pure-indexed.log`.
  Runtime init dropped to about `5.59s`; full post-reset indexed key count was
  `0`; remaining reset-source prepass was about `5.52s`
  (`cells.address` about `5.30s`, `cells.default_formula` about `0.22s`).
- Independent explorer report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee2a2-e8ab-7f81-a7d2-0f35d0239671-cells-runtime-init-audit.md`.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeout 90s env BOON_PROBE_CELLS_CANCEL=1 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Initial nested profile: runtime init about `44.54s`; first indexed derived initialization about `43.61s`; test body about `45.19s`. |
| `timeout 90s env BOON_PROBE_CELLS_CANCEL=1 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Field-level profile: `cells.value` about `33.74s`, `cells.address` about `6.28s`, `cells.default_formula` about `0.24s`; test body about `46.64s`. |
| `timeout 90s env BOON_PROBE_CELLS_CANCEL=1 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Reset-source-only experiment; still about `47.34s`, proving source dependencies also had to be initialized. |
| `timeout 90s env BOON_PROBE_CELLS_CANCEL=1 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Dependency-prefix experiment; still about `41.03s` because pure `cells.value` was still eagerly initialized. |
| `timeout 90s env BOON_PROBE_CELLS_CANCEL=1 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Lazy pure-indexed experiment; runtime init about `5.59s`, test body about `6.18s`; probes were removed afterward. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text cells_selected_input_list_find_materializes_single_row_storage pure_boon_cells_helpers_support_documented_arithmetic_ops pure_boon_cells_replacing_reference_removes_stale_dependents pure_boon_cells_fanout_recomputes_from_generic_read_index -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Fail | Command-form error: Cargo accepts only one test-name filter. No code failure. |
| Focused shell loop over `cells_escape_cancel_restores_uncommitted_draft_from_row_formula_text`, `cells_selected_input_list_find_materializes_single_row_storage`, `pure_boon_cells_helpers_support_documented_arithmetic_ops`, `pure_boon_cells_replacing_reference_removes_stale_dependents`, and `pure_boon_cells_fanout_recomputes_from_generic_read_index` | Pass | All five tests passed; individual test bodies were `7.77s`, `6.03s`, `8.21s`, `6.46s`, and `6.98s`. |
