## Native Cells Source-Intent Payload Resolver Probe

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T18:46:10+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_native_playground/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee5d6-9b3f-79d1-9306-b9102041d949-native-cells-source-routing-audit.json`

What happened:

- Investigated the full native Cells editing scenario failure after the full
  workspace gate showed native playground failures.
- Subagent audit independently confirmed the engine-side problem: native
  text-input dispatch can send raw source-intent payload paths to the runtime.
  The failing event has:
  - `source = cell.sources.editor.change`
  - `address = store.selected_input.address`
  - `target_text = store.selected_input.editing_text`
  - `text = store.selected_input.editing_tet`
- The runtime guard is correct to reject this event because
  `cell.sources.editor.change` is row-scoped for `cells` and the event carries
  neither row binding identity nor a resolved address.
- Added generic native-side resolver improvements:
  - focus acquisition resolves address/target payloads against a merged
    runtime payload summary rather than copying source-intent strings directly;
  - the merged payload summary deep-merges `state_summary()` and
    `document_state_summary()` so nested document fields such as
    `store.selected_input` are not discarded when `store` exists in both views;
  - data-path lookup now supports exact flat keys and singleton-list projection
    traversal;
  - route-table focused text resolves display text when it is actually a data
    binding path.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee5d6-9b3f-79d1-9306-b9102041d949-native-cells-source-routing-audit.json`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo check -p boon_native_playground --tests --quiet` | Pass | Focused native compile check passed; existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings only. |
| `cargo fmt --all` | Pass | Applied formatting after native resolver edits. |
| `timeout 180s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_native_playground cells_formula_bar_click_accepts_text_edit -- --nocapture` | Pass | Passed before the deep-merge fix in `45.78s`; proves the basic formula-bar editing path still works. |
| `timeout 210s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_native_playground cells_formula_bar_click_accepts_text_edit -- --nocapture` | Pass | Passed after singleton projection and deep-merge resolver changes in `51.00s`. |
| `timeout 90s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_native_playground cells_native_editing_scenario_updates_cell_and_formula_with_keyboard_navigation -- --nocapture` | Fail | Full scenario still failed at event 8 with unresolved `store.selected_input.*` payload paths. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_native_playground cells_native_editing_scenario_updates_cell_and_formula_with_keyboard_navigation -- --nocapture` | Fail | Repeated after resolver changes; still failed at event 8 with the same unresolved payload. |

Open findings:

- This slice is not complete. The focused formula-bar path passes, but the
  multi-step native Cells scenario still caches or regenerates raw
  source-intent payload paths before keyboard dispatch.
- The next fix should move payload normalization into a runtime-owned API, for
  example resolving `LiveSourceEvent` address/target/text binding payloads
  against current runtime state and evaluated `SourceBinding` metadata before
  row-scoped routing. Native code should not keep duplicating runtime summary
  traversal rules.
- The full native scenario is expensive: each run takes about `52s` after
  compile and can consume several CPU cores in the native test runtime. Use
  focused resolver tests or a bounded runtime-owned event-normalization unit
  test before rerunning the full scenario.

## Native Cells Source-Intent Payload Resolver Fix

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T19:32:24+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_native_playground/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee5f3-b59b-7530-922d-dc9e3dfdbe91-native-cells-source-binding-metadata-audit.json`

What happened:

- Added a second independent subagent audit for the formula-bar
  `cell.sources.editor.change` failure. The audit confirmed that source path
  lowering normalized `store.selected_input.sources.editor.events.change` to the
  runtime source path, but source-binding metadata extraction still read only
  the raw expression path and lost row identity.
- Fixed source-intent metadata extraction so event fields share normalized
  SourceBinding lookup and attach `list_id`, `target_key`,
  `target_generation`, `source_id`, and `bind_epoch` to source-intent
  assertions.
- Extended canonical `Element/...` event-record lowering to use the same
  expression-aware metadata path as direct document fields.
- Added a focus-cache refresh before repeatable text edits so cached address,
  target, and text payloads are resolved from the live runtime summary before a
  Backspace/typed character can mutate a raw data path literal.
- Found a second native dispatch bug: a Cells grid click could send
  `address = cell.sources.editor.select` because an explicit address intent
  resolved to the event source, while the concrete target was `C0`. Added an
  engine-side dispatch guard so an address payload equal to the event source
  falls back to the concrete target payload.
- Kept regression assertions in the full native Cells scenario proving:
  - C0 click selects `store.selected_address = "C0"`;
  - `store.selected_input.address` resolves to `C0` before formula editing;
  - focused text is not the raw `store.selected_input.editing_text` path before
    keyboard input.

## Phase 6/9 Cells PlanExecutor Delta Coalescing and Aggregate Refresh

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T20:15:14+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`

What happened:

- Reran the focused BYTES/MachinePlan aggregate in `--check-existing` mode after
  the native/runtime edits. It failed because current child evidence was bound
  to stale `boon_cli`/`xtask` binary hashes.
- Replayed the stale child commands from their stored `command_argv`. The first
  real semantic failure was `cells-plan-compare`.
- Root cause: PlanExecutor emitted transient indexed `FieldSet:value` semantic
  deltas inside one tick for Cells formula commits. The final state matched
  legacy, but the semantic delta stream included an internal `value = ""`
  before the final recomputed value for the same row/field.
- Fixed PlanExecutor delta publication by coalescing duplicate indexed
  `FieldSet` deltas by semantic target within a tick, keeping the last committed
  value while preserving non-duplicate order.
- Rebuilt stable `boon_cli`/`xtask` binaries, refreshed the binary-bound child
  reports, and reran the aggregate through `target/debug/xtask` directly to
  avoid Cargo relinking during validation.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-plan-compare.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- replay logs: `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/refresh-stable-*.log`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Fail | Found `42` schema-invalid child reports after binary changes. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-initial-run-plan.json` | Fail | Confirmed stale `boon_cli` binary hash. |
| `timeout 1200s bash -lc '... replay schema-invalid child reports ...'` | Fail | Stopped at `cells-plan-compare`; PlanExecutor/legacy semantic deltas differed while state matched. |
| `env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Clean after the runtime delta coalescing patch. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo build -p boon_cli` | Pass | Rebuilt the CLI with the runtime patch. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/boon_cli run examples/cells.bn --scenario examples/cells.scn --engine compare --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-plan-compare.json` | Pass | Cells PlanExecutor compare now has `state_match=true` and `semantic_delta_match=true`. |
| `timeout 180s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/boon_cli run examples/cells.bn --scenario examples/cells.scn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json` | Pass | Refreshed the non-compare Cells report. |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo build -p xtask -p boon_cli` | Pass | Stabilized both binaries before report replay. |
| `timeout 1200s bash -lc '... replay 40 schema-invalid reports with target/debug/xtask for xtask children ...'` | Pass | Refreshed the remaining binary-bound child reports without failures. |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Aggregate accepted all `43` required reports. Summary: `36` proof reports, `7` diagnostic reports, `33` PlanExecutor reports, `33` no-fallback PlanExecutor reports. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Aggregate report schema accepted. |
| `git diff --check` | Pass | No whitespace errors. |

Open findings:

- The focused BYTES/MachinePlan aggregate is current and green again, including
  Cells compare parity and no-fallback counters for the accepted PlanExecutor
  reports.
- Full workspace tests have not been rerun after this fix. The native
  playground failure cluster from the earlier workspace run remains mostly
  unresolved except for the specific Cells keyboard-editing failure fixed in the
  previous slice.
- The report refresh workflow is fragile when `cargo xtask` relinks binaries
  during validation. Prefer stabilizing binaries first and using
  `target/debug/xtask` for direct aggregate checks when refreshing binary-bound
  evidence.
- Phase 10 remains incomplete: default execution has not switched to
  PlanExecutor, release performance evidence is not final, and final
  independent adversarial audits are still open.

## Phase 6/9 Cells Delta Coalescing Regression Hardening

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T20:27:02+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee640-0aad-76d1-80c3-6c596fd1f1d1-plan-delta-coalescing-audit.json`

What happened:

- Added focused runtime regression coverage for the Cells semantic-delta
  coalescing bug:
  - `cells_plan_executor_coalesces_transient_indexed_value_deltas` runs the
    Cells expected-source-event compare path and asserts D0/J0 publish exactly
    one final `FieldSet:value` delta, not a transient empty value followed by
    the final value.
  - `plan_field_set_delta_coalescing_keeps_last_same_target_only` verifies the
    coalescer keeps only the last same-target `FieldSet`, keeps different row
    keys, and preserves non-`FieldSet` deltas.
- A fresh independent subagent audit reviewed the coalescing change. It agreed
  the key is well scoped for transient recompute deltas and recommended the
  helper-level test. It also raised a medium follow-up: if two indexed update
  branches can intentionally write different values to the same row field in one
  tick, the current coalescer would hide that conflict. That remains deferred to
  a dedicated duplicate indexed update conflict-policy test/fix because this
  slice addresses transient derived refresh deltas, not two user-authored
  conflicting update branches.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee640-0aad-76d1-80c3-6c596fd1f1d1-plan-delta-coalescing-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

The test now runs the existing Cells compare path on a 16 MiB thread stack and passed in `73.31s`. |

## Phase 6/9 Indexed Update Conflict Guard Follow-Up

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T20:29:00+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `examples/bytes_indexed_duplicate_update_conflict_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_indexed_duplicate_update_conflict_plan_ops.scn`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee64f-84f8-7690-b03e-6faedb903656-indexed-conflict-guard-audit.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What happened:

- Resolved the medium follow-up from the Cells delta-coalescing audit. The
  PlanExecutor now tracks real indexed update branch report rows before
  semantic deltas are appended to `pending_indexed_deltas`.
- The guard rejects any second real write to the same
  `list_id/key/generation/field_path` target in one event. It rejects both:
  - divergent values as conflicting indexed update branches;
  - same-value duplicate ownership as duplicate indexed update branches.
- The guard intentionally reads only `IndexedUpdateExecution.report_rows`, not
  `semantic_deltas`. Derived refresh churn can still be coalesced later, which
  preserves the Cells fix where transient `FieldSet:value` deltas are hidden
  but the final committed delta is published.
- A fresh independent subagent audit confirmed the boundary placement is right,
  identified the initial same-value duplicate gap, and recommended stronger
  PlanExecutor-level fixture coverage. The same-value duplicate gap is now
  resolved.
- Added `examples/bytes_indexed_duplicate_update_conflict_plan_ops.bn/.scn`, a
  real Boon negative fixture whose row-local `payload` field has two update
  branches from the same indexed source event. The focused runtime test proves
  this reaches PlanExecutor and is rejected as duplicate/conflicting indexed
  branch ownership instead of being hidden by semantic-delta coalescing.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee64f-84f8-7690-b03e-6faedb903656-indexed-conflict-guard-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

Commands run:

| --- | --- | --- |

| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime plan_field_set_delta_coalescing_keeps_last_same_target_only -- --nocapture` | Pass | Existing coalescer helper test still passed. |

## Phase 6/9 Duplicate LATEST Branch Compiler Rejection

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T21:19:49+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `examples/bytes_indexed_duplicate_update_conflict_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_indexed_duplicate_update_conflict_plan_ops.scn`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee662-bb13-74c2-8228-caa2d5fd3be1-duplicate-latest-branch-audit.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/child-report-refresh-duplicate-latest.log`

What happened:

- The duplicate indexed fixture initially exposed a compiler/IR smell: two
  direct `LATEST` branches from `receive.bytes` for `row.payload` lowered to a
  single IR/MachinePlan update branch. The PlanExecutor conflict guard was
  correct defense-in-depth, but the real authored duplicate never reached it.
- Added a typechecker diagnostic for duplicate direct `LATEST` `THEN` source
  triggers in the same `LATEST` body. This keeps the user-facing rule explicit:
  one source trigger owns one branch; ambiguous same-source ownership is a
  compiler error, not a silent first-branch choice.
- Added focused typecheck and IR regression tests. `boon_ir::lower` now rejects
  the real duplicate fixture before `boon_plan` can produce a misleading
  one-branch plan.
- Fixed the runtime negative test so `Ok(output)` panics instead of becoming a
  string that could satisfy substring assertions.
- Updated `verify-bytes-negative` and report-schema exact negative-case IDs so
  `plan-indexed-duplicate-update-branch-rejected` is now an honest
  `semantic-ir` rejection with the precise typecheck diagnostic.
- Ran a read-only subagent audit. It independently confirmed parser/typecheck
  acceptance plus IR source dedup/first-branch selection as the lossy boundary,
  and confirmed `boon_plan` was only mapping the already-lost branch set.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee662-bb13-74c2-8228-caa2d5fd3be1-duplicate-latest-branch-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/logs/child-report-refresh-duplicate-latest.log`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- dump-plan examples/bytes_indexed_duplicate_update_conflict_plan_ops.bn --report /tmp/duplicate-indexed-dump-current.json` | Pass before fix | Reproduced the bug: authored duplicate lowered to `commit_plan.update_branch_count=1`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck rejects_duplicate_direct_latest_then_source_branches -- --nocapture` | Pass | New typecheck regression rejects duplicate direct `LATEST` source branches. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck rejects_latest_branches_with_incompatible_data_types -- --nocapture` | Pass | Existing LATEST compatibility test still passes. |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- dump-plan examples/bytes_indexed_duplicate_update_conflict_plan_ops.bn --report /tmp/duplicate-indexed-dump-after-typecheck.json` | Fail as intended | Now fails with `duplicate direct LATEST branch for source receive.bytes`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_scalar_plan_executor_rejects_duplicate_indexed_update_branches -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed while still expecting the PlanExecutor string; passed after asserting the compiler diagnostic and panicking on `Ok(output)`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_report_schema bytes_negative_schema_requires_exact_case_ids -- --nocapture` | Pass | Schema exact negative-case list accepts the new ID. |
| `cargo test -p boon_ir lower_rejects_duplicate_direct_latest_source_branches -- --nocapture` | Pass | IR lowering rejects the real duplicate fixture. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | Formatter changed the new typecheck branch; final check passed after `cargo fmt --all`. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-negative --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json` | Pass | Negative gate now includes 39 required cases, including the duplicate branch as `semantic-ir`. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json` | Pass | Negative report schema-valid. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Fail, expected stale evidence | Failed because changing `boon_cli`/`xtask` made many child reports stale by `binary_hash`. |
| Child report refresh loop replaying 42 child `command_argv` entries except `phase0-baseline` | Pass | Regenerated binary-bound child reports. The log's final `refreshed 0` count is a shell subshell counter artifact; the numbered entries show reports 1 through 42 ran. |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Aggregate accepted all `43` required reports: `36` proof, `7` diagnostic, `33` PlanExecutor, `33` no-fallback PlanExecutor. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Aggregate report schema accepted. |

Open findings:

- This resolves the direct same-source `LATEST` ambiguity before IR/MachinePlan.
  It does not yet implement a general exclusivity proof for intentionally
  guarded same-source branches. Such branches should remain modeled as one
  source-owned branch with explicit match/guard semantics until a broader
  compiler proof exists.
- Full workspace tests, release performance reproduction, final adversarial
  stale/tamper audit, and Phase 10 default-engine switch readiness remain
  incomplete.

## Phase 7/9 BYTES Storage Counter Proof

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T21:52:16+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What happened:

- Added runtime-local BYTES storage counters for inline runtime byte values,
  `Bytes::copy_from_slice`, `Vec` clones, explicit `Vec` allocations, and
  zero-fill operations.
- Wired counter snapshots into the CPU root-scenario PlanExecutor after initial
  state warm-up. Reports now expose `plan_executor.bytes_storage_counters`,
  per-step `bytes_storage_counters`, and `bytes_storage_no_copy`.
- Added focused runtime assertions proving:
  - `Bytes/length` on fixed inline BYTES performs no measured byte-buffer copy
    in the measured tick;
  - `Bytes/concat` reports the currently allocated output buffer.
- Added `cargo xtask verify-bytes-storage-profile`, report-schema validation for
  that command, and the report as a required child of
  `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all`.
- Updated report-schema semantic replay comparison to ignore the new per-step
  counter metadata when comparing semantic PlanExecutor behavior. The dedicated
  storage-profile schema validates the counters themselves.

Measured counter evidence from `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Case | Expectation | no_copy | measured_copy_bytes | Counter detail |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `fixed-read-only-no-copy` | no runtime copy | `true` | `0` | all copy/clone/alloc/zero-fill byte counters are `0` |
| `concat-measured-copy` | measured copy | `false` | `10` | `vec_alloc_bytes=10`, `inline_value_count=2` |
| `slice-take-drop-measured-copy` | measured copy | `false` | `11` | `copy_from_slice_bytes=11`, `copy_from_slice_count=3` |
| `set-measured-clone` | measured copy | `false` | `4` | `vec_clone_bytes=4`, `vec_clone_count=1` |

## Phase 7 BYTES Slice View Optimization

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T22:13:50+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- added `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-bytes-slice-view-review.json`

What changed:

- Replaced measured `Bytes::copy_from_slice` use for `Bytes/slice`,
  `Bytes/take`, and `Bytes/drop` with checked `bytes::Bytes::slice` views in
  the root PlanExecutor measured path.
- Aligned the scalar replay/general BYTES helper path with the same view-slice
  behavior for inline BYTES.
- Preserved explicit bounds checks before `Bytes::slice` so invalid ranges
  return Boon/runtime errors instead of panicking.
- Changed the storage profile gate from
  `slice-take-drop-measured-copy` to `slice-take-drop-no-copy`.
- Tightened report-schema validation so storage profile reports must contain
  exact expected no-copy cases
  `fixed-read-only-no-copy`, `slice-take-drop-no-copy` and exact expected
  measured-copy cases `concat-measured-copy`, `set-measured-clone`.

Measured counter evidence from
`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Case | Expectation | no_copy | measured_copy_bytes | Counter detail |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `fixed-read-only-no-copy` | no runtime copy | `true` | `0` | all copy/clone/alloc/zero-fill byte counters are `0` |
| `concat-measured-copy` | measured copy | `false` | `10` | `vec_alloc_bytes=10`, `inline_value_count=2` |
| `slice-take-drop-no-copy` | no runtime copy | `true` | `0` | `copy_from_slice_bytes=0`, `vec_clone_bytes=0`, `vec_alloc_bytes=0`, `zero_fill_bytes=0`, `inline_value_count=3`, `inline_value_bytes=11` |
| `set-measured-clone` | measured copy | `false` | `4` | `vec_clone_bytes=4`, `inline_value_count=1` |

Before/after for the optimized case:

- Previous storage profile: `slice-take-drop-measured-copy` reported
  `measured_copy_bytes=11` and `copy_from_slice_bytes=11`.
- Current storage profile: `slice-take-drop-no-copy` reports
  `measured_copy_bytes=0` and all measured byte-buffer copy/clone/alloc/zero
  counters at `0`.
- `inline_value_bytes=11` still records the three logical BYTES values produced
  by slice/take/drop; it is not a byte-buffer copy counter.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-bytes-slice-view-review.json`

## Phase 7/9 MachinePlan Fixed BYTES Byte-Bank Metadata

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T22:47:58+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- added `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-byte-bank-layout-review.json`

What changed:

- Added `StorageLayout.byte_banks` metadata for fixed-length BYTES scalar
  storage slots.
- Added `ByteStorageBank` entries with typed plan/storage/state IDs, optional
  scope, indexed/root flag, fixed length, and capacity hint.
- Added `CapabilitySummary.byte_bank_storage_count`.
- Added plan verifier check `byte-bank-slots-match-fixed-bytes`.
- Added `cargo xtask verify-bytes-byte-bank-layout`, report-schema validation
  for that report, and the report as a required proof child of
  `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all`.
- Refreshed child reports after the MachinePlan serialization/hash changed so
  the aggregate binds to the current `boon_cli` and `xtask` binaries.
- Hardened `verify-bytes-byte-bank-layout` report-schema validation after
  independent review. The schema now validates the actual `byte_banks` payload,
  not only summary counts: source path, plan hash shape, payload length,
  required bank fields, root/indexed counts derived from payload, exact fixed
  lengths for the required cases, root capacity hints, and indexed `scope_id`.
- Added focused schema tamper coverage so missing banks, forged counts,
  incorrect fixed lengths, and incorrect indexed flags are rejected.

- Subagent `019ee6ca-2786-7cc1-8bdd-a11f47ca53a0`
  (`performance-storage-auditor`) reviewed the byte-bank code and report
  evidence without edits or heavy tests.
- Medium finding resolved: the original schema trusted byte-bank summary counts
  too much. The parent hardened schema validation and added the focused tamper
  regression listed above.
- Low finding deferred: a direct `boon_plan` verifier mutation test for
  missing/extra/mismatched bank metadata would still be useful. Current report
  schema tamper coverage protects the evidence path, and the direct verifier
  mutation test is deferred behind fixed-bank runtime execution work.
- Reviewer report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-byte-bank-layout-review.json`.

Operational note:

- Several proof gates are expensive even with `CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1` and
  `nice -n 19`. During this slice, `verify-bytes-file-read-plan`,
  `verify-bytes-storage-profile`, and the aggregate verifier each used about
  one CPU core for tens of seconds to roughly two minutes. They were bounded by
  `timeout`, but verifier performance should be treated as its own follow-up
  problem so routine evidence refreshes do not make the desktop feel hot.

Open findings:

- This slice declares and verifies MachinePlan byte-bank layout metadata only.
  The CPU PlanExecutor still executes BYTES through the current inline/private
  `RuntimeBytes` sidecar path.
- Fixed-byte bank execution, preallocated bank mutation, and zero-allocation
  fixed-byte normal tick proof after warm-up remain open Phase 7 work.
- `Bytes/concat` and `Bytes/set` remain measured-copy storage-profile cases.
- Full repeated release performance evidence, Cells release benchmark evidence,
  final adversarial stale/tamper audits, full workspace tests, and Phase 10
  default-engine switch readiness remain incomplete.

## Phase 7 Fixed Root Byte-Bank `Bytes/set` Execution

Recorded at: `2026-06-20T23:29:11+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/Cargo.toml`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-byte-bank-layout.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- added `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-set-review.json`

What changed:

- Added executor-private `RootFixedBytesBanks` for fixed root BYTES slots,
  initialized from typed constants before measured tick counters are sampled.
- Added fixed-bank validation helpers that keep public BYTES summaries aligned
  with private bank bytes.
- Added `RootFixedBytesMutation` so selected update branches can carry fixed
  bank mutation intent through conflict resolution and apply it at commit time.
- Changed root fixed `Bytes/set` to compute the patched digest without building
  an output byte buffer, then mutate/copy into the preallocated destination bank
  at commit.
- Taught root `ReadPath` and `PreviousValue` to preserve fixed-bank private
  bytes when copying BYTES values into fixed-bank outputs.
- Updated storage-profile gate/schema from `set-measured-clone` to
  `set-fixed-bank-no-copy`. `Bytes/concat` remains the measured-copy case.

Measured counter evidence from
`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Case | Expectation | no_copy | measured_copy_bytes | Counter detail |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `fixed-read-only-no-copy` | no runtime copy | `true` | `0` | all measured copy/clone/alloc/zero-fill byte counters are `0` |
| `concat-measured-copy` | measured copy | `false` | `10` | `vec_alloc_bytes=10`, `inline_value_count=2` |
| `slice-take-drop-no-copy` | no runtime copy | `true` | `0` | `copy_from_slice_bytes=0`, `vec_clone_bytes=0`, `vec_alloc_bytes=0`, `zero_fill_bytes=0`, `inline_value_count=3`, `inline_value_bytes=11` |
| `set-fixed-bank-no-copy` | no runtime copy | `true` | `0` | `copy_from_slice_bytes=0`, `vec_clone_bytes=0`, `vec_alloc_bytes=0`, `zero_fill_bytes=0`, `inline_value_count=0` |

- Subagent `019ee6d8-0b2a-79d3-8518-4a157e638546`
  (`performance-storage-auditor`) inspected the root BYTES PlanExecutor path.
- Reviewer highlighted the main invariant: public digest/length JSON, private
  bytes/bank state, semantic deltas, and legacy comparison must remain aligned.
- Reviewer report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-set-review.json`.

Open findings:

- This slice removes measured byte-buffer allocation/copy counters for the
  fixed root `Bytes/set` mutation tick. It does not yet make every subsequent
  BYTES operation consume borrowed fixed-bank views.
- When a later operation reads a bank-only mutated value, some paths can still
  materialize an inline `Bytes` copy until `Bytes/get`, `Bytes/length`,
  `Bytes/concat`, numeric reads/writes, and search predicates accept borrowed
  bank views directly.
- Fixed indexed byte-bank execution remains metadata-only.
- `Bytes/concat` still allocates a new output buffer and remains the measured
  copy storage-profile case.
- Full repeated release performance evidence, Cells release benchmark evidence,
  final adversarial stale/tamper audits, full workspace tests, and Phase 10
  default-engine switch readiness remain incomplete.

What changed:

- Added `RootBytesView` so root BYTES read operations can report whether they
  consumed borrowed private state or owned fallback bytes.
- Changed root `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/is_empty`, and `Bytes/get` to read
  through `RootBytesView` and include non-semantic `bytes_access` diagnostics in
  executed-update reports.
- Added `examples/bytes_set_read_bank_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_set_read_bank_plan_ops.scn`, where one step patches a fixed
  BYTES value and the next step only reads it with `Bytes/get` and
  `Bytes/length`.
- Added the `fixed-bank-read-after-set-no-copy` storage-profile case and schema
  requirements. The schema now requires the borrowed read proof to name the
  `inspect-bank` step, both update kinds, values `170` and `4`, zero byte
  counters, `access_source=root_fixed_byte_bank`, and `cow_kind=borrowed`.
- Adjusted source-derived semantic replay comparison to ignore `bytes_access`
  diagnostics. These diagnostics are runtime evidence and not part of semantic
  replay state.

## Phase 7 File/write_bytes Host-Boundary Borrowed BYTES Evidence

Status: partial Phase 7 storage/runtime evidence; complete for the current
root BYTES state `File/write_bytes` proof fixture.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed required `target/reports/bytes-plan/*.json` child reports
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-file-write-bytes-access-review.json`

What changed:

- `File/write_bytes` already used `root_state_bytes_view`, which returns a
  read-only borrowed `RootBytesState` view for the generated root BYTES
  fixture.
- The verifier and report schema were wrong: they expected
  `typed_initial_constant_fallback` / `owned`, which is the weaker fallback
  path that allocates an owned byte vector after private byte-state and fixed
  byte-bank lookup fail.
- Updated `verify-bytes-file-write-plan` and the schema validator to require
  `bytes_access.access_source == "root_bytes_state"` and
  `bytes_access.cow_kind == "borrowed"` for the current file-write fixture.

`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`:

| --- | --- |
| Report status | `pass` |
| `bytes_access.read_only` | `true` |
| `bytes_access.access_source` | `root_bytes_state` |
| `bytes_access.cow_kind` | `borrowed` |
| `bytes_access.host_boundary` | `file_write_bytes` |
| `bytes_access.byte_len` | `4` |
| `host_effect.byte_len` | `4` |
| `host_effect.sha256` | `5f78c33274e43fa9de5659265c1d917e25c03722dcb0b8d27db8d5feaa813953` |
| `bytes_storage_no_copy` | `true` |
| `copy_from_slice_bytes` | `0` |
| `vec_clone_bytes` | `0` |
| `vec_alloc_bytes` | `0` |
| `zero_fill_bytes` | `0` |

| --- | --- | --- |

| `cargo check -p boon_runtime -p xtask -p boon_report_schema --quiet` | Pass | Focused compile for changed crates; existing native GPU dead-code warnings only. |

  verifier expectation bug, not a runtime BYTES access bug.

  vector via `.to_vec()`.

  performance evidence, adversarial final audit, full workspace tests, and

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T00:35:40+02:00`

- `examples/bytes_set_numeric_bank_plan_ops.scn`

What changed:

- Changed root-scalar PlanExecutor `Bytes/read_unsigned` and
  `Bytes/read_signed` to consume `RootBytesView` instead of materializing
  `root_state_inline_bytes`.
- Added `bytes_access` diagnostics for these numeric read operations. The
  diagnostic records `read_only=true`, input state id, access source, and
  borrowed/owned view kind.
- Added `examples/bytes_set_numeric_bank_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_set_numeric_bank_plan_ops.scn`. The first step patches fixed
  bytes from `[01, 02, 03, 04]` to `[01, FE, 03, 04]`; the second step performs
  unsigned/signed numeric reads that prove the patched `FE` byte is observed.
- Added storage-profile case
  `fixed-bank-numeric-read-after-set-no-copy`. Schema validation now requires
  the `inspect-numeric` step, exact target values, `read_only` access
  diagnostics, `access_source=root_fixed_byte_bank`, `cow_kind=borrowed`, and
  zero measured byte copy/clone/alloc/fill counters.

Numeric values proven in the storage profile:

| Target | Operation | Expected value | Fixed-bank access proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `store.read_u16_be` | `bytes_read_unsigned` | `510` | input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed`; `read_only=true` |
| `store.read_u16_le` | `bytes_read_unsigned` | `65025` | input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed`; `read_only=true` |
| `store.read_i8` | `bytes_read_signed` | `-2` | input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed`; `read_only=true` |

## Phase 7 Fixed-Bank Numeric Writes After Bytes/set

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T01:09:22+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_set_numeric_write_bank_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_set_numeric_write_bank_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-byte-bank-layout.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- added `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-numeric-write-review.json`

What changed:

- Extended internal `RootFixedBytesMutation` from one optional byte patch to an
  ordered patch list. This keeps the existing fixed-byte-bank commit mechanism
  but makes it usable for multi-byte numeric writes.
- Added fixed root byte-bank fast paths for root-scalar PlanExecutor
  `Bytes/write_unsigned` and `Bytes/write_signed`. When the input state is a
  validated fixed byte bank and the output state also has a fixed byte bank,
  the executor computes patch bytes and mutates the fixed bank at commit time
  without cloning the BYTES input.
- Added `bytes_access` diagnostics for numeric writes:
  `read_only=false`, input/output state ids, `access_source=root_fixed_byte_bank`,
  `cow_kind=borrowed`, `mutation_kind=fixed_byte_bank_patches`, and
  `patch_count`.
- Added `examples/bytes_set_numeric_write_bank_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_set_numeric_write_bank_plan_ops.scn`. The scenario patches
  `01 02 03 04` to `01 FE 03 04`, applies an unsigned big-endian write to make
  `01 FE 12 34`, applies a signed little-endian write to make `FE FF 12 34`,
  then inspects the final bytes.
- Added storage-profile case
  `fixed-bank-numeric-write-after-set-no-copy`. Schema validation now requires
  both numeric write updates, exact patch mutation diagnostics, final inspect
  values, and zero measured byte copy/clone/alloc/fill counters across the
  write and inspect steps.

Measured counter evidence from
`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Case | Status | no_copy | measured_copy_bytes | Numeric write evidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `fixed-bank-numeric-write-after-set-no-copy` | `pass` | `true` | `0` | `write_update_count=2`, `write_kinds_match=true`, `borrowed_numeric_writes=true`, `step_zero_byte_counters=true` |

Numeric write values proven in the storage profile:

| Step | Operation | Target | Proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `write-unsigned` | `bytes_write_unsigned` | `store.patched` | borrowed `root_fixed_byte_bank` patch mutation, `patch_count=2`, output byte length `4` |
| `write-signed` | `bytes_write_signed` | `store.patched` | borrowed `root_fixed_byte_bank` patch mutation, `patch_count=2`, output byte length `4` |
| `inspect-written` | `bytes_to_hex` | `store.hex` | final bytes `feff1234` |
| `inspect-written` | `bytes_read_unsigned` | `store.read_u16_be` | final unsigned big-endian read `4660` at offset `2` |
| `inspect-written` | `bytes_read_signed` | `store.read_i16_le` | final signed little-endian read `-2` at offset `0` |

Aggregate evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `bytes_storage_profile.case_count=9`
  - `fixed-bank-numeric-write-after-set-no-copy.status=pass`
  - `fixed-bank-numeric-write-after-set-no-copy.bytes_storage_no_copy=true`
  - `fixed-bank-numeric-write-after-set-no-copy.measured_copy_bytes=0`
  - `borrowed_read_evidence.status=pass`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `required_report_count=45`
  - `checked_report_count=45`
  - `proof_report_count=38`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `plan_executor_report_count=33`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`
  - `blockers=null`

| Command | Status | Notes |

Independent review:

- Subagent `019ee73c-fd1e-7381-8336-87bf48d152dc`
  (`performance-storage-auditor`) inspected current numeric write support and
  identified that parser/typechecker/IR/MachinePlan/runtime execution existed,
  but root PlanExecutor numeric write branches cloned the input and had no
  storage-profile proof for fixed byte-bank mutation.
- Reviewer recommended a focused fixture with committed-step sequencing:
  `Bytes/set`, numeric writes, then inspect through reads/hex. The implemented
  fixture follows that shape and keeps same-event dependency scheduling out of
  this narrow proof.
- Reviewer report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-numeric-write-review.json`.

Open findings:

- Borrowed fixed-bank root reads and patch mutations are now proven for
  `Bytes/get`, `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/is_empty`, `Bytes/equal`, `Bytes/find`,
  `Bytes/starts_with`, `Bytes/ends_with`, `Bytes/read_unsigned`,
  `Bytes/read_signed`, `Bytes/to_text`, `Bytes/to_hex`, `Bytes/to_base64`,
  `Bytes/write_unsigned`, and `Bytes/write_signed` on focused root-scalar fixed
  bank scenarios.
- `Bytes/concat`, file writes, dynamic byte banks, and indexed byte banks still
  need direct borrowed-storage execution or explicit measured-copy rationale.
- This slice uses committed-step sequencing. Same-event BYTES dependency
  scheduling and fixed indexed byte banks remain open.
- This is still focused Phase 7 evidence. It does not satisfy the full Phase 7
  zero-allocation fixed-byte warm tick requirement, broad Phase 9 performance
  evidence, adversarial final audit, full workspace tests, or Phase 10 default
  switch readiness.

## Phase 7 Slice/Take/Drop View-Backed Output Evidence

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T01:24:43+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

What changed:

- Added `bytes_access` output diagnostics for root-scalar PlanExecutor
  `Bytes/slice`, `Bytes/take`, and `Bytes/drop`.
- The diagnostics record `output_storage_kind=bytes_slice_view`,
  `output_cow_kind=borrowed_view`, input/output state ids, offset, byte count,
  and output byte length. This makes the existing `bytes::Bytes::slice` view
  behavior visible in reports.
- Added `slice_take_drop_view_evidence` to the storage-profile verifier for
  the existing `slice-take-drop-no-copy` case.
- Tightened report-schema validation so `slice-take-drop-no-copy` must include
  exact evidence for `store.sliced`, `store.taken`, and `store.dropped`, not
  only zero byte-buffer counters.

Measured counter evidence from
`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Case | Status | no_copy | measured_copy_bytes | View evidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `slice-take-drop-no-copy` | `pass` | `true` | `0` | `slice_update_count=3`, `output_slice_views=true`, `step_zero_byte_counters=true` |

Slice/take/drop values proven in the storage profile:

| Target | Operation | Offset | Byte count | Output byte length | Output proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `store.sliced` | `bytes_slice` | `1` | `3` | `3` | `bytes_slice_view` / `borrowed_view` |
| `store.taken` | `bytes_take` | `0` | `4` | `4` | `bytes_slice_view` / `borrowed_view` |
| `store.dropped` | `bytes_drop` | `2` | `4` | `4` | `bytes_slice_view` / `borrowed_view` |

## Phase 7 Borrowed Fixed-Bank Predicate Reads After `Bytes/set`

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T00:19:15+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_set_predicates_bank_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_set_predicates_bank_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-byte-bank-layout.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- added `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-predicate-read-review.json`

What changed:

- Changed root-scalar PlanExecutor `Bytes/equal`, `Bytes/find`,
  `Bytes/starts_with`, and `Bytes/ends_with` to consume `RootBytesView` instead
  of materializing `root_state_inline_bytes`.
- Added per-input `bytes_access` diagnostics for these read-only predicates.
  The diagnostic records the operand role, state id, access source, and whether
  the view is borrowed or owned.
- Added `examples/bytes_set_predicates_bank_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_set_predicates_bank_plan_ops.scn`. The first step patches a
  fixed BYTES value to include byte `16uAA`; the second step runs equality,
  find, prefix, and suffix predicates whose expected values depend on that
  patched byte.
- Added storage-profile case
  `fixed-bank-predicates-after-set-no-copy`. Schema validation now requires the
  `inspect-predicates` step, five predicate updates, exact target values, a
  `read_only` access diagnostic, a borrowed `root_fixed_byte_bank` input for
  every predicate update, and zero measured byte copy/clone/alloc/fill counters.

Measured counter evidence from
`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Case | Status | no_copy | measured_copy_bytes | Borrowed predicate evidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `fixed-bank-predicates-after-set-no-copy` | `pass` | `true` | `0` | `inspect-predicates` has 5 predicate updates; `targets_match=true`, `values_match=true`, `borrowed_predicate_reads=true`, `step_zero_byte_counters=true` |

Predicate values proven in the storage profile:

| Target | Operation | Expected value | Fixed-bank access proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `store.same` | `bytes_equal` | `true` | one input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed`; both byte inputs are borrowed |
| `store.found_index` | `bytes_find` | `2` | haystack is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed`; both byte inputs are borrowed |
| `store.missing_index` | `bytes_find` | `null` | haystack is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed`; both byte inputs are borrowed |
| `store.starts` | `bytes_starts_with` | `true` | input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed`; both byte inputs are borrowed |
| `store.ends` | `bytes_ends_with` | `true` | input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed`; both byte inputs are borrowed |

Aggregate evidence:

  - `status=pass`
  - `bytes_storage_profile.case_count=6`
  - `fixed-bank-predicates-after-set-no-copy.status=pass`
  - `fixed-bank-predicates-after-set-no-copy.bytes_storage_no_copy=true`
  - `fixed-bank-predicates-after-set-no-copy.measured_copy_bytes=0`
  - `borrowed_read_evidence.status=pass`

Independent review:

- Subagent `019ee70d-efe1-7aa3-9185-abfa36fa999a`
  (`performance-storage-auditor`) inspected the current BYTES predicate read
  paths and recommended replacing `root_state_inline_bytes` with
  `root_state_bytes_view` only for the four read-only root-scalar predicate
  branches.
- Reviewer specifically warned that checking only one arbitrary borrowed input
  can produce a false proof. The new fixture and schema therefore require
  values that depend on the patched `16uAA` byte plus a borrowed fixed-bank
  input on every predicate update.

  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-predicate-read-review.json`.

  `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/is_empty`, `Bytes/equal`, `Bytes/find`,
  `Bytes/starts_with`, and `Bytes/ends_with` on the focused root-scalar fixed
  bank surface.
- `Bytes/concat`, file writes, dynamic byte
  banks, and indexed byte banks still need direct
  borrowed-storage execution or explicit measured-copy rationale.
- The global BYTES storage counters are not isolated across parallel Rust unit
  tests. Counter-sensitive tests should run single-threaded or be refactored to
  use per-test/per-run counter scopes.
- This is still focused Phase 7 evidence. It does not satisfy the full Phase 7
  zero-allocation fixed-byte warm tick requirement, broad Phase 9 performance
  evidence, adversarial final audit, full workspace tests, or Phase 10 default
  switch readiness.

## Phase 7 Borrowed Fixed-Bank Conversion Reads After Bytes/set

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T00:51:03+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_set_conversion_bank_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_set_conversion_bank_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-byte-bank-layout.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- added `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-conversion-read-review.json`

What changed:

- Changed root-scalar PlanExecutor `Bytes/to_text`, `Bytes/to_hex`, and
  `Bytes/to_base64` to consume `RootBytesView` instead of materializing
  `root_state_inline_bytes`.
- Added per-update `bytes_access` diagnostics for these read-only conversions.
  The diagnostic records the input state id, `read_only=true`,
  `access_source`, and `cow_kind`.
- Added `examples/bytes_set_conversion_bank_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_set_conversion_bank_plan_ops.scn`. The first step patches a
  fixed BYTES value from `ABC` to `AZC`; the second step converts the patched
  fixed byte-bank state to UTF-8 text, hex, and base64.
- Added storage-profile case
  `fixed-bank-conversions-after-set-no-copy`. Schema validation now requires
  the `inspect-conversions` step, three conversion updates, exact target
  values, read-only borrowed `root_fixed_byte_bank` access for every conversion
  update, and zero measured byte copy/clone/alloc/fill counters.

Measured counter evidence from
`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Case | Status | no_copy | measured_copy_bytes | Borrowed conversion evidence |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `fixed-bank-conversions-after-set-no-copy` | `pass` | `true` | `0` | `inspect-conversions` has 3 conversion updates; `targets_match=true`, `values_match=true`, `borrowed_conversion_reads=true`, `step_zero_byte_counters=true` |

Conversion values proven in the storage profile:

| Target | Operation | Expected value | Fixed-bank access proof |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `store.text` | `bytes_to_text` | `AZC` | input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed` |
| `store.hex` | `bytes_to_hex` | `415a43` | input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed` |
| `store.base64` | `bytes_to_base64` | `QVpD` | input is `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed` |

Aggregate evidence:

Independent review:

- Subagent `019ee72f-5688-7383-8d66-5e4b61951cee`
  (`performance-storage-auditor`) inspected the conversion proof shape and
  confirmed the fixture should patch `ABC` to `AZC`, then read the same state
  through `Bytes/to_text`, `Bytes/to_hex`, and `Bytes/to_base64`.
- Reviewer specifically called out that text/hex/base64 string allocation is
  outside the runtime BYTES storage counters; the accepted proof is that the
  BYTES input read is borrowed from `root_fixed_byte_bank` with zero measured
  runtime BYTES storage copy counters.
- Reviewer report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-conversion-read-review.json`.

Open findings:

- Borrowed fixed-bank root reads are now proven for `Bytes/get`,
  `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/is_empty`, `Bytes/equal`, `Bytes/find`,
  `Bytes/starts_with`, `Bytes/ends_with`, `Bytes/read_unsigned`,
  `Bytes/read_signed`, `Bytes/to_text`, `Bytes/to_hex`, and
  `Bytes/to_base64` on the focused root-scalar fixed bank surface.
- `Bytes/concat`, file writes, dynamic byte
  banks, and indexed byte banks still need direct
  borrowed-storage execution or explicit measured-copy rationale.
- This conversion fixture covers UTF-8 `Bytes/to_text`. ASCII uses the same
  borrowed view path but is not separately covered after `Bytes/set`.
- This is still focused Phase 7 evidence. It does not satisfy the full Phase 7
  zero-allocation fixed-byte warm tick requirement, broad Phase 9 performance
  evidence, adversarial final audit, full workspace tests, or Phase 10 default
  switch readiness.

## Phase 7 Fixed-Bank File/write_bytes After Bytes/set

Status: partial Phase 7 storage/runtime evidence; complete for root fixed-byte
bank input to `File/write_bytes` after a prior `Bytes/set` mutation.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`

- refreshed stale `xtask` child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-bank-file-write-review.json`

What changed:

- Added a generated storage-profile fixture under
  `target/generated/bytes-storage-profile-file-write-fixtures/`.
- The fixture runs two ordered steps:
  - `patch-bytes`: `Bytes/set` changes `01 02 03 04` to `01 02 AA 04`.
  - `write-patched-file`: `File/write_bytes` writes `store.patched` to
    `outputs/patched.bin`.
- Added storage-profile evidence
  `fixed-bank-file-write-after-set-no-copy`. The evidence requires the write
  update, `root_fixed_byte_bank` / `borrowed` host-boundary access, exact
  artifact digest/bytes, and zero runtime BYTES storage copy counters.
- Added schema validation for this case. The schema requires legacy comparison
  to stay enabled for every other storage-profile case and to be explicitly
  disabled only for this host-boundary `File/write_bytes` proof, matching the
  standalone file-write gate.

## Phase 7/9 Large Dynamic Source-Payload File/write_bytes Shared Storage

Status: partial Phase 7 runtime/storage and Phase 9 verification evidence;
complete for the focused root dynamic SOURCE payload -> PlanExecutor BYTES
state -> `File/write_bytes` host-boundary path when the payload is larger than
the inline threshold and must use shared runtime storage.

Base/current commit: `6494b54e488dc63cfb39d0e1d50830f1283b80dc`

Files changed:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-large-dynamic-file-write-shared-review.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed stale `xtask` child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/goal-readiness.json`

What changed:

- `verify-bytes-storage-profile` now generates a second dynamic
  `File/write_bytes` fixture:
  - source:
    `target/generated/bytes-storage-profile-file-write-fixtures/bytes_dynamic_large_file_write_plan_ops.bn`
  - scenario:
    `target/generated/bytes-storage-profile-file-write-fixtures/bytes_dynamic_large_file_write_plan_ops.scn`
  - receive step: `receive-large-dynamic-bytes`
  - write step: `write-large-dynamic-file`
  - output: `outputs/dynamic-large.bin`
- The payload is 1025 bytes, so it is above
  `SOURCE_EVENT_INLINE_BYTES_LIMIT=1024`. It uses first byte `0xDE`, last byte
  `0xEF`, and `0x7E` filler.
- `dynamic_file_write_source_payload_evidence` is now data-driven instead of
  hardcoding the previous 3-byte inline case. It checks expected byte length,
  digest, storage kind, step IDs, output path, final state summary, host
  effect, artifact bytes, borrowed root BYTES access, and zero measured byte
  counters.
- Report schema now requires
  `dynamic-large-file-write-source-payload-shared-no-copy`, requires
  `storage == "shared"` for that case, and validates the 1025-byte artifact and
  borrowed `root_bytes_state` host-boundary access.

Status: partial Phase 9 adversarial verification evidence; complete for

- `verify-bytes-machine-plan-adversarial` now has five required aggregate
  tamper cases for the large dynamic shared storage-profile proof:
  - `aggregate-storage-profile-large-dynamic-state-summary-tamper-rejected`
  - `aggregate-storage-profile-large-dynamic-shared-storage-tamper-rejected`
  - `aggregate-storage-profile-large-dynamic-byte-len-tamper-rejected`
  - `aggregate-storage-profile-large-dynamic-host-digest-tamper-rejected`
  - `aggregate-storage-profile-large-dynamic-bytes-access-len-tamper-rejected`
- The adversarial mutations target the required
  `dynamic-large-file-write-source-payload-shared-no-copy` case in
  `bytes-storage-profile.json`.
- The report schema now requires the five new adversarial IDs.
- The storage-profile schema now directly validates public
  `case.state_summary.store.received` fields, `store.write_status`,
  `host_effect.artifact_path`, host effect status/mode/write mode,
  `verified_after_write=true`, artifact listing in `artifact_sha256s`, and
  emitted `bytes_access.input_state_id` plus `bytes_access.path_source`.

`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-adversarial.json`:

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Report status | `pass` |
| `adversarial_case_count` | `14` |
| Large dynamic state summary tamper | rejected |
| Large dynamic shared storage tamper | rejected |
| Large dynamic byte length tamper | rejected |
| Large dynamic host digest tamper | rejected |
| Large dynamic bytes-access length tamper | rejected |

Representative rejection reasons:

- State summary tamper: `state_summary does not match dynamic file-write
  evidence`.
- Shared storage and byte-length tamper: `receive update did not prove dynamic
  BYTES source payload`.
- Host digest tamper: `host_effect did not match dynamic artifact`.
- Bytes-access length tamper: `dynamic file-write update did not prove borrowed
  root-bytes host-boundary access`.

## Phase 7 Malformed Private BYTES Artifact Runtime Safety

Status: partial Phase 7 runtime-safety evidence; complete for focused
malformed private `RuntimeBytes::from_artifact` rejection without panics.

Base/current commit: `6494b54e488dc63cfb39d0e1d50830f1283b80dc`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-malformed-private-bytes-artifact-review.md`

What changed:

- Added
  `runtime_bytes_from_artifact_rejects_malformed_private_state`.
- The test table-drives malformed private BYTES artifact payloads and wraps
  every decode in `std::panic::catch_unwind`.
- Each malformed case must return a structured `Err`, not panic and not decode
  successfully.
- Covered malformed artifacts:
  - missing `inline_bytes`;
  - non-array `inline_bytes`;
  - non-byte `inline_bytes` entry;
  - `inline_bytes` entry above `255`;
  - declared `byte_len` mismatch;
  - empty digest;
  - unsupported storage kind.
- Updated the existing
  `root_scalar_plan_executor_rejects_static_out_of_bounds_bytes_slice` test to
  assert the current earlier typecheck diagnostic instead of the older
  MachinePlan verifier message. The engine now rejects this static OOB fixture
  before plan execution, which is stricter than the old expectation.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime runtime_bytes_from_artifact_rejects_malformed_private_state -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | First run failed because the new test referenced an `xtask`-local panic helper. After replacing it with local panic-message extraction, the test passed. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime bytes_runtime_builtins_report_deterministic_errors -- --nocapture` | Pass | Existing deterministic BYTES builtin error test still passes. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_scalar_plan_executor_rejects_static_out_of_bounds_bytes_slice -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | First run exposed that the compiler now rejects the static OOB slice at typecheck with a precise bounds diagnostic. The test expectation was updated and passed. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting check after runtime/doc edits. |
| `cargo check -p boon_runtime --quiet` | Pass | Focused runtime compile. |

Independent review:

- Subagent `019eedf0-4150-7ff1-bd07-604c3ea9385c`
  (`runtime safety auditor`) identified malformed private BYTES artifact decode
  as the smallest useful Phase 7 safety gap.
- The reviewer recommended a focused `boon_runtime` unit test because the
  decoder is a private runtime boundary and would otherwise require an indirect
  public test hook.
- Reviewer report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-malformed-private-bytes-artifact-review.md`.

Open findings:

- This closes one focused malformed private-state decode boundary.
- Broader malformed in-memory runtime-state tests, dynamic runtime OOB matrices,
  zero-allocation fixed-byte warm ticks, broad Phase 9 performance evidence,
  postponed `TASK-0804A`, full workspace tests, and Phase 10 default-switch
  readiness remain open.

## 2026-06-22 - Phase 7 Dynamic Shared BYTES Storage For Large Payloads

Status: partial Phase 7 runtime/storage and Phase 9 verification evidence;
complete for large runtime-owned source-payload and dynamic-path
`File/read_bytes` payloads using executable shared storage instead of inline
runtime storage.

Base/current commit: `6494b54e488dc63cfb39d0e1d50830f1283b80dc`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-dynamic-shared-bytes-review.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- refreshed stale xtask-owned child reports and
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- Added `RuntimeBytesPayload::Shared(Bytes)` for runtime-owned dynamic BYTES
  larger than the current 1024 byte source-event inline threshold.
- Added dynamic constructors that keep small runtime-owned buffers as `inline`
  and store larger runtime-owned buffers as `shared`.
- `RuntimeBytes::inline_bytes()` now accepts both `Inline` and `Shared`
  executable payloads. `BlobRef` and `PageRef` still reject executable byte
  operations because they are descriptor-only until a resolver exists.
- Root and generic source-payload BYTES ingestion now use the dynamic
  constructor, so large source payloads are not reintroduced as inline runtime
  state after being artifact-backed in reports.
- Root and indexed PlanExecutor `File/read_bytes` now use the dynamic
  constructor, so large file-read results become shared executable runtime
  BYTES.
- Report-schema replay now expects `shared` summaries for source-payload and
  dynamic file-read BYTES values above the inline threshold.
- `verify-bytes-file-read-plan` now generates a 1025 byte dynamic-path file-read
  fixture and requires update/state storage to be `shared` while proving later
  `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get` consume the value correctly.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - source-event payload storage remains `artifact`
  - PlanExecutor update value storage is `shared`
  - `byte_len=1025`
  - `bytes_storage_no_copy=true`
  - measured BYTES helper counters for the step:
    `copy_from_slice_bytes=0`, `vec_clone_bytes=0`, `vec_alloc_bytes=0`,
    `zero_fill_bytes=0`, and `inline_value_bytes=0`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - dynamic-path file-read fixture `byte_len=1025`
  - dynamic-path update storage is `shared`
  - final `store.file_bytes.storage=shared`
  - follow-up `store.file_len=1025`
  - follow-up `store.first_byte=222`
  - check `dynamic-path-large-file-read-uses-shared-storage` passed
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass=true`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`

## 2026-06-22 - Phase 3 Static Text-to-BYTES Conversion Refinement

Recorded at: `2026-06-22T06:48:03+02:00`

Status: partial Phase 3 type-system evidence; complete for static literal
TEXT/BYTES conversion length refinement and invalid static conversion
diagnostics in the covered v1 conversion builtins.

Base/current commit: `6494b54e488dc63cfb39d0e1d50830f1283b80dc`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase3-static-conversion-review.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-adversarial.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- refreshed stale xtask-owned child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`

What changed:

- Static literal `Text/to_bytes(encoding: Utf8)` now refines to fixed BYTES
  length using UTF-8 byte length.
- Static literal `Text/to_bytes(encoding: Ascii)` now refines to fixed BYTES
  length when the literal is ASCII and emits a typechecker diagnostic when a
  static literal contains non-ASCII text.
- Static literal `Bytes/from_hex` now refines to fixed decoded BYTES length
  when the literal is valid hex text and emits a typechecker diagnostic for
  odd-length or invalid static hex text.
- Static literal `Bytes/from_base64` now refines to fixed decoded BYTES length
  when the literal is valid base64 text and emits a typechecker diagnostic for
  invalid static base64 text.
- Dynamic text inputs intentionally remain `BYTES[dynamic]`; their malformed
  data remains a deterministic runtime/plan error instead of a compiler error.
- The paired same-message negative cases for invalid static hex/base64 are now
  independent fixtures so one diagnostic cannot satisfy two required IDs.

  - `negative_case_count=83`
  - required static conversion IDs:
    - `typecheck-static-text-to-bytes-ascii-rejects-non-ascii`
    - `typecheck-static-bytes-from-hex-rejects-odd-digits`
    - `typecheck-static-bytes-from-hex-rejects-invalid-digits`
    - `typecheck-static-bytes-from-base64-rejects-bad-length`
    - `typecheck-static-bytes-from-base64-rejects-invalid-digit`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass=true`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/goal-readiness.json`
  - `status=fail`
  - remaining blockers:
    - `8 phases are still partial in the progress ledger`
    - `1 phases are still not started in the progress ledger`
    - `Cells release benchmark wrapper report is missing because TASK-0804A remains blocked by speed budgets`
    - `Phase 10 default switch has not happened; boon_cli run still defaults to legacy`

## 2026-06-22 - Phase 3 Static Fixed-BYTES Bounds Diagnostics

Status: partial Phase 3 type-system evidence; complete for statically known
fixed-BYTES out-of-bounds diagnostics in the covered v1 builtin families.

Base/current commit: `6494b54e488dc63cfb39d0e1d50830f1283b80dc`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase3-static-bounds-review.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-adversarial.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- refreshed 44 stale child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/` from
  their recorded `command_argv` values after rebuilding `boon_cli` and `xtask`

What changed:

- BYTES builtin argument validation now receives the actual piped input
  expression ID rather than only a boolean pipe flag. This lets the typechecker
  inspect fixed-BYTES input lengths while preserving call-form validation.
- The typechecker now emits compile-time diagnostics when all required facts
  are static:
  - `Bytes/get(index: literal)`
  - `Bytes/set(index: literal, value: ...)`
  - `Bytes/slice(offset|start: literal, byte_count|length|count: literal)`
  - `Bytes/take(byte_count|length|count: literal)`
  - `Bytes/drop(byte_count|length|count: literal)`
  - `Bytes/read_unsigned`, `Bytes/read_signed`
  - `Bytes/write_unsigned`, `Bytes/write_signed`
- Signed numeric literals are considered for bounds diagnostics, so `index: -1`
  and negative offsets/counts are rejected as statically known out-of-bounds
  mistakes.
- Static-bounds input lookup ignores invalid positional BYTES call arguments.
  Positional args still produce the existing ambiguity diagnostic, but no longer
  cascade into misleading static-bounds diagnostics.
- `docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md` now states the convention explicitly:
  statically known fixed-BYTES OOB is a typechecker diagnostic; dynamic OOB
  remains a deterministic runtime/plan error.
- `verify-bytes-negative` now requires 78 negative cases. New schema-required
  typecheck IDs cover static positive OOB, negative OOB, signed numeric OOB,
  and the previous plan-level static slice/set cases now expect the earlier
  typechecker diagnostics.

Report evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `negative_case_count=78`
  - required static-bounds IDs include:
    `typecheck-bytes-get-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-get-negative-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-set-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-slice-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-slice-negative-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-take-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-drop-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-read-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-write-static-out-of-bounds`,
    `typecheck-bytes-read-signed-static-out-of-bounds`, and
    `typecheck-bytes-write-signed-static-out-of-bounds`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass=true`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/goal-readiness.json`
  - `status=fail`
  - remaining blockers:
    - `8 phases are still partial in the progress ledger`
    - `1 phases are still not started in the progress ledger`
    - `Cells release benchmark wrapper report is missing because TASK-0804A remains blocked by speed budgets`
    - `Phase 10 default switch has not happened; boon_cli run still defaults to legacy`

## 2026-06-22 - Phase 2 Parser Closure And Adversarial Gate Cost Fix

Status: Phase 2 parser surface is complete. The full BYTES/MachinePlan goal is
still incomplete because Phases 0-1 and 3-9 remain partial, Phase 10 is not
started, and `TASK-0804A` still blocks the Cells release benchmark wrapper.

BYTES/MachinePlan worktree.

- `crates/boon_parser/src/lib.rs`

- Added parser coverage for supported explicit byte-literal bases:
  `2u10101010`, `8u377`, `10u255`, and existing hexadecimal forms.
- Added exact-span assertions for a full `BYTES[2] { 16uAA, 10u7 }`
  constructor and the nested `16uAA` byte literal.
- Extended malformed BYTES parser cases for unsupported bases, missing digits,
  and unterminated constructor bodies.
- Extended `verify-bytes-negative` and report-schema expectations with:
  `parser-unsupported-byte-base`, `parser-byte-missing-digits`, and
  `parser-missing-closing-body`.
- Fixed the adversarial verifier cost shape. The old adversarial path ran the
  full 47-report aggregate once per aggregate tamper case. A single aggregate
  pass took about 36-70 seconds on this worktree, so the adversarial gate could
  run for minutes at one full CPU core with no output.
- The adversarial tamper runner now validates only the mutated required child
  report against the aggregate-facing contract: presence, report schema,
  `status=pass`, command, measurement mode, current git, artifact hash
  freshness, and no-fallback PlanExecutor counters when present. The real
  `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing` aggregate gate is unchanged.
- Added a process-local file-hash cache in `xtask`, keyed by path, length, and
  modified timestamp, so stable large artifacts such as `target/debug/xtask`
  are not rehashed repeatedly inside one verifier process while rewritten child
  reports still invalidate correctly.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `negative_case_count=63`
  - parser negative cases include:
    `parser-invalid-hex-byte`, `parser-multiline-invalid-hex-byte`,
    `parser-byte-overflow`, `parser-unsupported-byte-base`,
    `parser-byte-missing-digits`, `parser-bad-size-expression`,
    `parser-missing-body`, `parser-missing-closing-body`, and
    `parser-multiline-trailing-token`.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-adversarial.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `adversarial_case_count=9`
  - required cases include missing child report, stale child artifact hash,
    edited child success flag, runtime AST fallback, executable string-path,
    unknown plan op, indexed private BYTES summary tamper, hardware-bounded
    unbounded BYTES rejection, and fake benchmark profile rejection.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass=true`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`

Timing evidence:

- Pre-fix independent subagent diagnostic: one non-tampering aggregate pass took
  `36.09s` at `99%` CPU, and the old adversarial verifier multiplied that by
  seven aggregate tamper cases.
- Pre-targeting tactical cache run: standalone adversarial gate completed in
  `1:39.60` at `99%` CPU.
- Post-targeting final run: standalone adversarial gate completed in `0:38.00`
  at `100%` CPU.
- Final aggregate run completed in `1:10.41` at `100%` CPU.

Independent review:

- Subagent `019eed19-075b-7eb1-8977-ae96385d93ca`
  (`Zeno the 4th`) independently diagnosed the adversarial verifier cost. The
  reviewer found that `verify_bytes_machine_plan_adversarial` called
  `verify_bytes_machine_plan_all_with_required` for every tamper case, causing
  full report-schema and artifact-hash validation of all required reports for
  each case.
- The reviewer recommended targeted child validation for adversarial negative
  cases and a hash-cache/fail-fast mitigation. The implementation follows that
  direction while leaving the real aggregate gate unchanged.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_parser bytes -- --nocapture` | Pass | Four focused BYTES parser tests passed, including exact spans and malformed syntax diagnostics. |
